specs.title: snapshot(includeInactive:true) node-path set equals coir's static tree (modulo scene-root prefix)
specs.setup: ReadScene authored + saved; copse preview connected and reloaded onto ReadScene.
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__snapshot {includeInactive:true, relevant:false}  → collect every .ref.  coir: mcp__coir__tree {file:'ReadScene.scene'} → collect every node path.
specs.groundTruth: coir's static node hierarchy of the SAME .scene file (independent reader: coir parses the serialized prefab/scene JSON; copse walks the running preview tree).
specs.expect: Set of copse refs == set of coir node paths after stripping coir's scene-root prefix; same-name siblings carry [i] on both sides (Item[0], Item[1]). No node present in one and absent in the other.

specs.title: snapshot(includeInactive:true) node set equals cocos-creator editor hierarchy
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded in editor and in the copse preview.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator editor's own scene hierarchy readback (independent: editor in-memory scene graph, not copse's walk).
specs.expect: Every editor node (below the scene root) maps to exactly one copse ref and vice versa; copse's top level is 'Canvas' (scene root not emitted as a segment).

specs.title: Same-name siblings get 0-based [i]; unique names stay bare
specs.setup: ReadScene has two sibling nodes both named 'Item' and otherwise-unique names.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator scene_get_scene_hierarchy shows exactly two children named 'Item' under Canvas (independent count of same-name siblings).
specs.expect: Exactly refs 'Canvas/Item[0]' and 'Canvas/Item[1]' present (count == cocos-creator's count of 'Item' children); 'Canvas/ScoreLabel' bare (no [i]); no bare 'Canvas/Item'.

specs.title: Slim descriptor: no `name` key, `active` omitted when true, `components` opt-in only
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; ScoreLabel & AddBtn are active.
specs.action: mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:false} → inspect the descriptor for ref 'Canvas/AddBtn' and 'Canvas/ScoreLabel'.
specs.groundTruth: Structural contract of snapshot() in src/core/index.js (desc.ref only; active omitted unless false; components gated by {components:true}). No external oracle.
specs.expect: Descriptors have key 'ref' but NOT 'name'; active nodes have no 'active' key; no 'components' key anywhere (default snapshot).

specs.title: snapshot surfaces active:false for a node authored inactive
specs.setup: Author Canvas/Panel active=false via mcp__cocos-creator__node_set_node_property {property:'active', value:false}; save; mcp__copse__reload.
specs.groundTruth: The authored value (false), independently confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info active for Panel.
specs.expect: Panel descriptor has active===false; an active sibling (e.g. Canvas/ScoreLabel) descriptor has no 'active' key.

specs.title: snapshot({components:true}) type list matches cocos-creator's component list (dev preview unminified)
specs.setup: ReadScene in editor + preview; preview is the dev/preview build (constructor.name is the real class name, not release-minified).
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__snapshot {components:true, relevant:false} → components[] for ref 'Canvas/ScoreLabel'.  oracle: mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_components for node ScoreLabel.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator's authoritative component list for the node (independent editor read).
specs.expect: copse components[] (stripped of 'cc.') equals cocos-creator's component class set for ScoreLabel (includes Label + UITransform). Default snapshot (no flag) has no 'components' key.

specs.title: snapshot button descriptor carries authored interactable (true vs false)
specs.setup: AddBtn authored cc.Button interactable=true; DisabledBtn authored interactable=false; preview reloaded.
specs.action: mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:false} → descriptors for 'Canvas/AddBtn' and 'Canvas/DisabledBtn' (note: DisabledBtn requires includeInactive only if inactive; it is active here).
specs.groundTruth: The authored cc.Button.interactable values, confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {component:'cc.Button'} for each node.
specs.expect: AddBtn: button===true, interactable===true, click is an array; DisabledBtn: button===true, interactable===false.

specs.title: snapshot label field equals authored Label.string
specs.setup: Author Canvas/ScoreLabel cc.Label.string="0" via cocos-creator; reload preview.
specs.groundTruth: Authored string "0", confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {component:'cc.Label'}.string.
specs.expect: ScoreLabel descriptor .label === "0" (string type preserved).

specs.title: onlyInteractive/interactive() returns exactly the button nodes
specs.setup: ReadScene has 4 active buttons (AddBtn, DisabledBtn, TwoEventBtn, TouchBtn).
specs.action: mcp__copse__interactive {} → collect refs.  oracle: coir find/tree count of nodes carrying cc.Button (mcp__coir__tree {file:'ReadScene.scene', with:'cc.Button'}).
specs.groundTruth: coir's static count/list of cc.Button-bearing nodes in the same scene (independent).
specs.expect: Set of interactive() refs == set of coir cc.Button node paths (modulo root prefix); every returned descriptor has button===true; no non-button node appears.

specs.title: relevant:true keeps only button|label|codeHandlers nodes, drops bare containers/plain nodes
specs.setup: ReadScene: AddBtn (button), ScoreLabel (label), Canvas (bare container), Canvas/Item[0] (plain node, no label).
specs.action: Compare mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:true} refs vs mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:false} refs.
specs.groundTruth: snapshot keep-predicate contract (keep iff button|label!=null|codeHandlers) — structural; the membership of each node in those categories is confirmed by the authored fixture / cocos-creator component list.
specs.expect: relevant set includes 'Canvas/AddBtn' and 'Canvas/ScoreLabel'; excludes bare 'Canvas' and plain 'Canvas/Item[0]'; relevant set is a subset of the full (relevant:false) set.

specs.title: includeInactive toggles the hidden subtree; delta equals the authored-inactive subtree
specs.setup: Canvas/Panel authored active=false with active child PanelChild; preview reloaded.
specs.action: A = mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:false}.refs ; B = mcp__copse__snapshot {includeInactive:true, relevant:false}.refs ; compute B\A.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator hierarchy: Panel(active=false) and its descendants are exactly the inactive subtree (independent editor read).
specs.expect: B\A == {'Canvas/Panel','Canvas/Panel/PanelChild'} (the authored inactive subtree); default snapshot A contains neither.

specs.title: reachable field is opt-in: absent from default snapshot even though runtime supports it
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; runtime supplies rt.reachable (real cc preview).
specs.action: mcp__copse__snapshot {relevant:false} (default reachability off) → button descriptor for 'Canvas/AddBtn'; compare to mcp__copse__interactive {} (reachability on).
specs.groundTruth: snapshot's reachability gate contract (reachable only emitted when {reachability:true} AND rt.reachable exists). Structural.
specs.expect: default-snapshot AddBtn descriptor has NO 'reachable'/'blockedBy'/'occludedBy'/'visible' keys; interactive() AddBtn descriptor DOES carry 'reachable'.

specs.title: codeHandlers attached to a snapshot descriptor only when the node has user node.on() listeners
specs.setup: Canvas/TouchBtn carries CopseListener (registers one user node.on(TOUCH_END) in onLoad); other buttons have none. Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: The authored CopseListener script source (we KNOW it registers exactly one user TOUCH_END handler); AddBtn has no custom listener script.
specs.expect: TouchBtn descriptor has codeHandlers array containing a {type:'touch-end'} entry; AddBtn descriptor has no 'codeHandlers' key (Button's own touch listeners are filtered out).

specs.title: clickSurface emits one (ref,method) row per editor-wired clickEvent; method joins 1:1 to coir's static map
specs.setup: AddBtn wired with one clickEvent → CopseCounter.add; TwoEventBtn wired with two (add, reset). Preview reloaded.
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__click_surface {} → rows.  oracle: coir static ClickEvent map for the same buttons (mcp__coir__get on each cc.Button clickEvents, or coir's click→method edges).
specs.groundTruth: coir's static ClickEvent handler names for the SAME .scene (independent serialized read of clickEvents[].handler).
specs.expect: Rows for AddBtn: exactly one with method==='add'; TwoEventBtn: two rows method==='add' and method==='reset' (same ref); each method equals coir's serialized handler name for that button.

specs.title: A button with empty clickEvents yields a single row with method:null
specs.setup: Canvas/TouchBtn authored cc.Button with clickEvents=[] (no editor-wired handlers). Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator component_get_component_info {component:'cc.Button'}.clickEvents length==0 for TouchBtn (independent confirmation the button has zero serialized clickEvents).
specs.expect: Exactly one row for TouchBtn with method===null (and no extra rows); confirms a button outside coir's static ClickEvent surface.

specs.title: A method:null row carries codeHandlers ride-along (lets the join call it code-registered, not bare code-only)
specs.setup: TouchBtn has empty clickEvents AND CopseListener (one user node.on listener). Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored CopseListener registers exactly one user listener (independent script-source oracle).
specs.expect: TouchBtn row: method===null AND has a non-empty codeHandlers array (the live node.on listener rides along).

specs.title: clickSurface rows carry omit-default flags (interactable) reflecting the authored button
specs.setup: AddBtn interactable=true, DisabledBtn interactable=false (DisabledBtn has a clickEvent so it produces a row). Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored cc.Button.interactable values (confirmed by cocos-creator component_get_component_info).
specs.expect: AddBtn row interactable===true; DisabledBtn row interactable===false; flags follow omit-default (interactable present because it is read off the descriptor).

specs.title: clickSurface drops non-button nodes (no rows for Labels/plain nodes)
specs.setup: ReadScene has ScoreLabel (label-only) and Item (plain).
specs.groundTruth: clickSurface contract: only descriptors with d.button produce rows. Structural.
specs.expect: No row has ref 'Canvas/ScoreLabel' or 'Canvas/Item[0]'/'Canvas/Item[1]'; every emitted row corresponds to a node carrying a cc.Button.

specs.title: clickSurface `component` (handler class) matches coir's class on dev preview; documented-minified on release
specs.setup: AddBtn clickEvent component is CopseCounter; running against the unminified dev preview.
specs.action: mcp__copse__click_surface {} → AddBtn row .component.  oracle: coir's static handler-class for that clickEvent.
specs.groundTruth: coir's serialized component/handler-class name for the same clickEvent (coir keeps the real class name even when release builds minify it).
specs.expect: On the dev preview AddBtn row .component==='CopseCounter' and equals coir's class; spec documents that on a minified release build this field is mangled and the JOIN must use `method` instead.

specs.title: get path:Label.string returns the exact authored string (flagship authored-readback)
specs.setup: Author Canvas/ScoreLabel cc.Label.string="42" via mcp__cocos-creator__component_set_component_property; save; mcp__copse__reload.
specs.groundTruth: The value authored through cocos-creator MCP ("42"), confirmed by component_get_component_info — the WRITE path is the editor, the READ path is copse (independent).
specs.expect: {ok:true, value:"42"} with value of string type (=== "42", not coerced).

specs.title: get path:CopseCounter.count returns the authored numeric field with type fidelity
specs.setup: CopseCounter attached to Canvas/Counter with count=5 (authored via component default / set_component_property); preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: The authored field value 5 (number), confirmed by cocos-creator component_get_component_info for CopseCounter.count.
specs.expect: {ok:true, value:5} where value is the number 5 (not the string "5").

specs.title: get path:Node.active (pseudo-component) reads the authored node intrinsic
specs.setup: Canvas/Panel authored active=false; preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored node.active===false, confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info active for Panel.
specs.expect: {ok:true, value:false} (boolean). The 'Node' pseudo-component routes through rt.readProp(node,member), not getComponent.

specs.title: get path:Node.name equals the node's editor name
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded.
specs.action: mcp__copse__get {sel:'Canvas/ScoreLabel:Node.name'}.  oracle: mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info name for ScoreLabel.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator's node name (independent editor read).
specs.expect: {ok:true, value:'ScoreLabel'} matching the editor name (and the ref's last segment).

specs.title: get on Item[1] reads the SECOND same-name sibling's authored value (disambiguation correctness)
specs.setup: Canvas/Item[1] (the second 'Item') carries a cc.Label string="second"; Item[0] has none. Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored: the editor's 2nd 'Item' child has Label.string="second"; confirmed by cocos-creator reading the i-th child's component.
specs.expect: Item[1] → {ok:true, value:'second'}; Item[0] → {ok:false, reason:'no-component'} (no Label). Confirms [i] picks the correct sibling by child order.

specs.title: get on a missing node returns {ok:false, reason:'not-found'} (not a throw)
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; no node named 'Ghost'.
specs.action: mcp__copse__get {sel:'Canvas/Ghost:Label.string'} and an out-of-range index mcp__copse__get {sel:'Canvas/Item[9]:Label.string'}.
specs.groundTruth: resolve() returns null for an absent path / out-of-range [i]; get short-circuits to reason:'not-found'. Structural guard.
specs.expect: Both return {ok:false, reason:'not-found'} (and the MCP wrapper does NOT mark isError — a clean negative, not an exception).

specs.title: get path:Comp.prop where the component is absent returns {ok:false, reason:'no-component'}
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel has a Label but no Sprite.
specs.groundTruth: rt.getComponent returns null for an absent component → reason:'no-component' (confirmed: cocos-creator component_get_components for ScoreLabel has no cc.Sprite). Structural guard.
specs.expect: {ok:false, reason:'no-component'} (distinct from not-found — the node exists, the component does not).

specs.title: get an existing component but a non-existent member returns {ok:true, value:undefined}
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel has cc.Label.
specs.groundTruth: rt.readProp(c,member) returns undefined for an absent member; get still reports ok:true. Structural guard (the contract distinguishes missing-component from missing-member).
specs.expect: {ok:true, value:undefined} (ok stays true; value is undefined/null over the JSON bridge).

specs.title: Malformed member selector (no ':' or no '.') surfaces an error via the MCP tool (engine error → isError)
specs.surface: get splitMember throw → server.js isError mapping
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded.
specs.groundTruth: splitMember throws ('selector needs ":Comp.member"' / '"Comp.member" after :'); the MCP server maps the thrown error to an isError tool result. Structural guard.
specs.expect: Both calls return an MCP error result (isError) whose message mentions ':Comp.member' / 'Comp.member' — NOT a silent {ok:false} and NOT a hang.

specs.title: node() returns the documented intrinsic shape (active/activeInHierarchy/scale/worldPos/size, opacity only with UIOpacity, NO onScreen)
specs.setup: Canvas/Sized has UITransform contentSize + UIOpacity; Canvas/Item[0] has neither UIOpacity.
specs.groundTruth: nodeInfo contract in src/cocos/runtime.js (opacity emitted only when a UIOpacity component exists; size only when UITransform.contentSize; no onScreen field). Structural.
specs.expect: Sized result has keys ok,ref,active,activeInHierarchy,scale,worldPos,size,opacity; Item[0] has the same minus 'opacity' (no UIOpacity) and minus 'size' if no UITransform; neither has 'onScreen'.

specs.title: node() distinguishes active (self) from activeInHierarchy (inherited): active child under inactive parent
specs.setup: Canvas/Panel authored active=false; its child PanelChild authored active=true. Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored active flags, confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info (active AND activeInHierarchy) for both nodes — independent editor read.
specs.expect: PanelChild: active===true but activeInHierarchy===false (inactive ancestor); Panel: active===false, activeInHierarchy===false. Matches cocos-creator's two fields exactly.

specs.title: node().size equals the authored UITransform.contentSize
specs.setup: Canvas/Sized authored cc.UITransform contentSize width=200 height=100 via cocos-creator; preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored contentSize {200,100}, confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {component:'cc.UITransform'} contentSize.

specs.title: node().opacity equals the authored UIOpacity.opacity and is absent without a UIOpacity
specs.setup: Canvas/Sized authored cc.UIOpacity opacity=128; Canvas/ScoreLabel has no UIOpacity. Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored UIOpacity.opacity=128, confirmed by component_get_component_info; ScoreLabel has no cc.UIOpacity (component_get_components).
specs.expect: Sized.opacity===128; ScoreLabel result has NO 'opacity' key (conditional emission).

specs.title: node().scale equals the authored node scale
specs.setup: Author Canvas/Sized scale (x=2,y=3) via mcp__cocos-creator__node_set_node_transform; preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored scale, confirmed by mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info scale (independent editor read).

specs.title: node().worldPos (rounded) matches cocos-creator's worldPosition
specs.setup: Canvas/Sized authored at a known position; preview reloaded.
specs.action: mcp__copse__node {ref:'Canvas/Sized'} → .worldPos.  oracle: mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info worldPosition for Sized.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator's worldPosition for the same node (independent editor read).
specs.expect: worldPos.x===Math.round(editorWorldX) and worldPos.y===Math.round(editorWorldY) (copse rounds to int; allow that exact rounding, not a free tolerance).

specs.title: node() on a missing ref returns {ok:false, reason:'not-found'}
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; no 'Ghost' node.
specs.groundTruth: resolve()===null → reason:'not-found'. Structural guard.
specs.expect: {ok:false, reason:'not-found'} (no throw, not isError).

specs.title: listeners() surfaces the user node.on() handler with {type,fn?,target?}, Button's own touch filtered out
specs.setup: Canvas/TouchBtn carries cc.Button + CopseListener (registers exactly one user node.on(TOUCH_END) in onLoad). Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored CopseListener script source: registers exactly one user TOUCH_END listener; cc.Button's own touch listeners are filtered by target-identity. Independent oracle = the script we wrote.
specs.expect: Returns an array with exactly one entry of type==='touch-end' (the user listener); the Button's internal touch-start/end/cancel and engine-internal events do NOT appear.

specs.title: listeners() on a plain button (only Button's own listeners) returns []
specs.setup: Canvas/AddBtn is a plain cc.Button with no custom script registering node.on().
specs.groundTruth: cc.Button registers its own touch listeners which codeHandlers filters by target identity, leaving no USER handlers. Independent: AddBtn has no listener script (fixture authored without one).
specs.expect: Returns [] (empty array) — Button's own listeners filtered, no user handlers present.

specs.title: listeners() on a missing ref returns null
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; no 'Ghost' node.
specs.groundTruth: install.listeners: resolve returns null → returns null (distinct from [] which means 'node exists, no user listeners'). Structural guard.
specs.expect: Returns null (NOT []).

specs.title: MCP snapshot tool defaults relevant:true (narrower than the library default relevant:false)
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded with bare containers (Canvas) and plain nodes (Item) plus buttons/labels.
specs.groundTruth: tools.js run(): relevant defaults to (a.relevant ?? true) — the MCP default differs from the core library default (false). Structural contract.
specs.expect: No-arg MCP snapshot excludes bare 'Canvas'/'Canvas/Item[0]' (relevant filter on by default); {relevant:false} includes them. The no-arg result is a strict subset.

specs.title: MCP click_surface defaults reachability:true (rows carry reachable) unless reachability:false
specs.setup: ReadScene loaded; runtime supplies rt.reachable.
specs.action: mcp__copse__click_surface {} vs mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:false} → inspect AddBtn row keys.
specs.groundTruth: install.clickSurface: reachability is on unless (opts.reachability)===false. Structural contract (the reachable VALUE belongs to the reachability lens; here we only assert presence/absence of the field).
specs.expect: Default rows carry a 'reachable' key; {reachability:false} rows omit 'reachable'/'blockedBy'/'visible'. (Field presence only — value correctness is the reachability lens's spec.)

specs.title: get returns correct JS primitive types over the bridge (bool/number/string distinguished)
specs.surface: get value type fidelity (puppeteer evaluate serialization)
specs.setup: ScoreLabel.string="0" (string), Label.fontSize=40 (number) authored; Panel.active=false (bool) authored. Preview reloaded.
specs.groundTruth: Authored values + their types (string "0", number 40, boolean false), confirmed by cocos-creator readbacks.
specs.expect: value "0" is a string (=== "0", not 0); fontSize value is the number 40 (typeof number); active value is boolean false — types not coerced to strings by the JSON bridge.

specs.title: press a clickEvent-wired button increments the bound counter 0->1
specs.setup: Fixture authored + saved via cocos-creator MCP; copse connected to the preview (mcp__copse__connect {url:'http://localhost:7456'}); state normalized via call CopseCounter.setCount(0).
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.setCount","args":[0]}  2) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> baseline  3) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/AddBtn"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> after
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic fixture source: CopseCounter.add() does this.count++ and the clickEvent is wired to add(), so predicted after=baseline+1=1. Editor confirms the authored baseline independently via mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {node:'Canvas/Mgr', component:'CopseCounter'} (count===0 before any press). NOTE: editor reads the edit-mode scene, a SEPARATE runtime from the preview copse mutates, and debug_execute_script is non-functional in this rig — so the mutation oracle is the deterministic source, not the editor.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:true, ref:'Canvas/AddBtn', fired:1, drove:['clickEvent']} (no `wired`/`touched` keys); after-baseline===1 exactly (not 0 = press did nothing; not 2 = double-fire).

specs.title: press updates the bound Label string in lockstep with the count field (two independent copse surfaces agree)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: add() sets count=1 AND scoreLabel.string=String(count)='1'. The count field (read via getComponent('CopseCounter').count) and the Label.string (read via getComponent('Label').string) are TWO independent copse resolver/read paths over the SAME logical state — a resolver/read bug would desync them. Oracle = predicted '1' on both.
specs.expect: step3 value===1 (number) AND step4 value==='1' (string) — both equal the predicted post-state; agreement rules out a get mis-resolution.

specs.title: two presses accumulate: count 0->2 (each press fires the handler exactly once)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: add() increments by exactly 1 per call; two presses -> predicted count=2. Each press result independently reports fired:1.
specs.expect: both press results fired:1/drove:['clickEvent']; final count===2 (proves each press actuated exactly once — not 1=second press lost, not 3+/4=spurious extra fire).

specs.title: a button with TWO serialized clickEvents reports fired:2 and applies both (count+=2)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0). Canvas/Add2Btn has two clickEvents both -> CopseCounter.add.
specs.groundTruth: core fireClickHandlers returns hs.length and emits EACH; the fixture wires two add() clickEvents -> predicted fired:2 and count delta=+2. coir's static ClickEvent map for Canvas/Add2Btn (mcp__coir__get / find) independently shows TWO click->add edges, cross-confirming the wired multiplicity.
specs.expect: press result fired===2, drove:['clickEvent']; count===2; coir reports 2 ClickEvent edges on Add2Btn (the wired count copse `fired` claims).

specs.title: press on interactable:false button is a no-op: reason 'disabled', zero mutation
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0). Canvas/DisabledBtn authored interactable:false, wired to add().
specs.action: 1) call setCount [0]  2) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> baseline  3) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/DisabledBtn"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> after
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic: a disabled button's handler is NOT fired, so predicted delta=0. Editor independently confirms the button's authored interactable:false via mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {node:'Canvas/DisabledBtn', component:'cc.Button'} (interactable===false). Predicted no-op = the external oracle (a wrongly-fired press would read baseline+1).
specs.expect: press -> {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/DisabledBtn', reason:'disabled'}; after===baseline (count unchanged); editor shows Button.interactable===false.

specs.title: press {force:true} fires a disabled button's handler -> count 0->1
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0). Same Canvas/DisabledBtn.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic: force skips the interactable gate, so add() runs once -> predicted count=1. Pair with DELTA-press-disabled-honored-noop (same button, force off = 0) so the only variable is the force flag.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:true, fired:1, drove:['clickEvent']}; count===1 (and ===baseline+1 vs the no-force no-op spec).

specs.title: press auto-attaches `changed.labelChanged` for the bound Label (0->1)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0). settle on (default).
specs.groundTruth: Driver mutate() snapshots {relevant,includeInactive} before+after, settles, and coreDiff -> labelChanged for nodes whose label value changed. Deterministic source: ScoreLabel goes '0'->'1'. The `changed` block is computed by copse's OWN diff path but the predicted from/to ('0'/'1') is the external oracle from the fixture source.
specs.expect: press result.changed.labelChanged contains {ref:'Canvas/ScoreLabel', from:'0', to:'1'} (exact from/to); changed present only because something changed.

specs.title: press OpenPanelBtn activates Canvas/Panel and reports it in `changed.activated`
specs.setup: Connected; Canvas/Panel authored active:false; OpenPanelBtn wired to PanelController.open.
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__node {"ref":"Canvas/Panel"} -> activeInHierarchy:false (precondition)  2) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/OpenPanelBtn"} (inspect result.changed)  3) mcp__copse__node {"ref":"Canvas/Panel"} -> activeInHierarchy:true
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: open() sets panel.active=true (panel present in BOTH before/after via includeInactive, so coreDiff classifies it activated not appeared). Predicted: Panel active false->true. node() intrinsic readback (a different copse surface than the snapshot diff) cross-confirms activeInHierarchy flipped.
specs.expect: step1 activeInHierarchy===false; press result.changed.activated includes a descriptor with ref:'Canvas/Panel'; step3 activeInHierarchy===true.

specs.title: press CloseBtn deactivates the open Panel -> `changed.deactivated`
specs.setup: Connected; Panel opened first (press OpenPanelBtn).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: close() sets panel.active=false -> predicted Panel active true->false. The Node pseudo-component get (Canvas/Panel:Node.active) independently confirms the flip via readProp, distinct from the snapshot-diff path.
specs.expect: step2 result.changed.deactivated includes ref:'Canvas/Panel'; step3 value===false.

specs.title: press a code-on('click')-only button: drove:['click'], no synthetic touch, codeCount 0->1
specs.surface: press (emitClick reaches on('click'); droveClick suppresses emitTouch)
specs.setup: Connected; Mgr.codeCount=0. Canvas/CodeClickBtn has NO clickEvents, a CopseWiring node.on('click') -> Mgr.codeBump().
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.setCount","args":[0]} (and reset codeCount via a reset method)  2) mcp__copse__listeners {"ref":"Canvas/CodeClickBtn"} (confirms a 'click' code handler is present)  3) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/CodeClickBtn"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.codeCount"}
specs.groundTruth: core press: fired=0 (no serialized clickEvents) but code has a type:'click' so droveClick=true -> drove:['click'], and emitTouch is SKIPPED (touched stays false). Deterministic source: the on('click') handler bumps codeCount -> predicted 1. copse listeners() independently shows the 'click' registration that emitClick targets.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:true, fired:0, drove:['click']} with NO `touched` and NO `wired` key; codeCount===1; listeners() included a {type:'click'} entry.

specs.title: press a touch-only button synthesizes a tap: drove:['touch'], touched:true, wired:true, touchCount 0->1
specs.setup: Connected; Mgr.touchCount=0. Canvas/TouchBtn has NO clickEvents, a CopseWiring node.on('touch-end') -> Mgr.touchBump().
specs.action: 1) reset touchCount (call a reset)  2) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/TouchBtn"} (inspect drove/touched/wired)  3) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.touchCount"}
specs.groundTruth: core press: fired=0, no on('click') -> falls back to rt.emitTouch which dispatches touch-start then touch-end at the node's screen centre; the TOUCH_END handler bumps touchCount. wired:true because the node HAS a code handler (clickHandlers empty but code.length>0). Predicted touchCount=1 from the deterministic handler. (Best-effort: if emitTouch returns false on this engine version, touched:false — flag for verify, not a hard contract.)
specs.expect: press -> drove:['touch'], touched:true, fired:0, wired:true; touchCount===1 (confirms the synthetic tap actually reached TOUCH_END).

specs.title: press a button wired to NOTHING: drove:'nothing', wired:false, zero mutation
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0). Canvas/DeadBtn = cc.Button, no clickEvents, no code handlers.
specs.action: 1) call setCount [0]  2) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> baseline  3) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/DeadBtn"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> after
specs.groundTruth: core press: fired=0, no click code, emitTouch finds no handler effect -> drove='nothing'; wired computed false (clickHandlers empty AND code empty). coir independently shows DeadBtn has NO ClickEvent edges (mcp__coir__get) and copse listeners() shows []. Predicted mutation delta = 0.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:true, fired:0, drove:'nothing', wired:false}; after===baseline; coir shows zero clickEvents on DeadBtn and copse listeners('Canvas/DeadBtn') === [].

specs.title: press a button with BOTH a clickEvent and an on('click'): drove:['clickEvent','click'], both effects apply
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0), codeCount(0). Canvas/BothBtn: clickEvent -> CopseCounter.add AND node.on('click') -> Mgr.codeBump().
specs.action: 1) call setCount [0]; reset codeCount  2) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/BothBtn"}  3) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.codeCount"}
specs.groundTruth: core press: fired>0 (clickEvent) pushes 'clickEvent'; droveClick true pushes 'click'; touch NOT synthesized (fired>0). Deterministic source: add() -> count=1 AND codeBump() -> codeCount=1. Both effects are predicted; touch absence prevents a double count.
specs.expect: press drove deep-equals ['clickEvent','click'], fired:1, no `touched`; count===1 AND codeCount===1 (exactly one of each — not 2).

specs.title: press a button whose clickEvent handler THROWS surfaces as an error (not a silent pass)
specs.surface: press (fireClickHandlers propagates throw) + driver errors / MCP isError
specs.setup: Connected. Canvas/ThrowBtn -> CopseCounter.boom() which throws Error('copse-test-boom').
specs.groundTruth: core fireClickHandlers calls h.emit which propagates the throw; in-page it becomes a console error / pageerror captured out-of-band over CDP into logs. Deterministic source: boom() throws the exact message 'copse-test-boom'. Oracle = that message appears as an error, never swallowed to ok:true with no signal.
specs.expect: Either the MCP press tool returns isError:true, OR press result.errors contains an entry whose text includes 'copse-test-boom'; logs() shows the same error line. A bare {ok:true} with no error is a FAIL.

specs.title: press a handler that catches its own throw and console.errors: ok:true/fired:1 yet result.errors is populated
specs.surface: press -> driver mutate() error capture (the swallowed-throw case)
specs.setup: Connected. Canvas/SwallowBtn -> CopseCounter.swallow() (try/catch -> console.error('copse-swallow ...')).
specs.groundTruth: Driver mutate() marks the log buffer index before the action and attributes any console-error/pageerror during the action+settle window to it. Deterministic source: swallow() logs 'copse-swallow copse-test-swallow' but does NOT rethrow, so core press returns ok:true/fired:1. The independent CDP console capture (page.on('console')) is the oracle that a passing fired:1 is NOT actually clean.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:true, fired:1, drove:['clickEvent']} AND result.errors present with text containing 'copse-swallow' (a passing fired count does not hide the swallowed error).

specs.title: press a non-existent ref returns not-found and mutates nothing
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0).
specs.action: 1) call setCount [0]  2) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> baseline  3) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/NoSuchBtn"}  4) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> after
specs.groundTruth: core press resolve()->null returns {ok:false, reason:'not-found'} before any fire. Contract guard (no external value); the no-op is also confirmed by after===baseline.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/NoSuchBtn', reason:'not-found'}; after===baseline (no global state touched).

specs.title: press a node that has no cc.Button returns not-a-button and mutates nothing
specs.setup: Connected. Canvas/ScoreLabel is a Label-only node (no Button).
specs.groundTruth: core press asButton()->null returns {ok:false, reason:'not-a-button'} before firing. The Label string is unchanged. coir/editor independently show no Button component on ScoreLabel.
specs.expect: press -> {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/ScoreLabel', reason:'not-a-button'}; Label.string unchanged.

specs.title: call CopseCounter.buy(30) returns 70 and mutates gold 100->70
specs.setup: Connected; gold reset to 100 (call a reset/setGold or reload).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: buy(n){this.gold-=n; return this.gold;} with gold=100, n=30 -> returns 70 and gold becomes 70. The call's return value AND the subsequent get are two independent observations of the same predicted post-state. Editor can replicate the SAME call on the edit-mode instance via mcp__cocos-creator__sceneAdvanced_execute_component_method {node:'Canvas/Mgr', component:'CopseCounter', method:'buy', args:[30]} -> also 100->70.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:70}; after get===70 (call return and field readback agree on the predicted value).

specs.title: call CopseCounter.setCount(9) drives count and bound Label to 9 (args array passed through)
specs.setup: Connected.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: setCount(9) sets count=9 and scoreLabel.string='9'. Predicted post-state 9 on both surfaces; confirms the MCP `args` array reaches the method positionally.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:undefined}; count===9 AND Label.string==='9'.

specs.title: call CopseCounter.add2(3,4) returns 7 and adds 7 to count (multiple positional args)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic source: add2(a,b){this.count+=a+b; return a+b;} -> returns 7, count 0->7. Confirms args[] spreads in order (callMethod c[m](...args)).
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:7}; count===7.

specs.title: call on a non-function member returns value undefined without throwing or mutating
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(5). 'count' is a data field, not a method.
specs.groundTruth: runtime callMethod: typeof c[m]==='function' ? c[m](...) : undefined — so invoking a data field yields value:undefined and does NOT call/throw/mutate. Deterministic: count stays 5.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:undefined} (no isError); count still===5.

specs.title: call addressing a missing component returns no-component (no mutation)
specs.setup: Connected. Canvas/Mgr has no cc.Sprite.
specs.groundTruth: core call getComponent()->null returns {ok:false, reason:'no-component'} before any call.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/Mgr:Sprite.setColor', reason:'no-component'}.

specs.title: call on a non-existent path returns not-found
specs.setup: Connected.
specs.groundTruth: core call resolve()->null returns {ok:false, reason:'not-found'}.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/Ghost:CopseCounter.add', reason:'not-found'}.

specs.title: call a method that throws surfaces as an MCP error (isError), not ok:true
specs.setup: Connected. CopseCounter.boom() throws.
specs.groundTruth: core call has no try/catch -> the thrown error propagates out of the in-page evaluate; the MCP server turns the rejected call into a tool result with isError:true and/or the driver attaches result.errors. Deterministic message 'copse-test-boom' is the oracle.
specs.expect: The MCP call tool result is isError:true OR result.errors contains 'copse-test-boom'; not a clean {ok:true,value:...}.

specs.title: call a method that catches its throw and console.errors: ok:true value undefined yet result.errors populated
specs.setup: Connected. CopseCounter.swallow() try/catch console.error.
specs.groundTruth: swallow() does not rethrow -> core call returns {ok:true,value:undefined}; the driver's CDP console capture attributes the console.error('copse-swallow ...') to this call window. The independent console channel is the oracle that the clean ok:true is not actually error-free.
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:undefined} AND result.errors present with text containing 'copse-swallow'.

specs.title: call PanelController.open() attaches `changed.activated` for Canvas/Panel (call mutate auto-diff)
specs.setup: Connected; Panel active:false.
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Panel:Node.active"} -> false  2) mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:PanelController.open","args":[]} (inspect result.changed)  3) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Panel:Node.active"} -> true
specs.groundTruth: call goes through the same driver mutate() wrapper as press, so a state change attaches `changed`. Deterministic source: open() sets panel.active=true -> Panel activated (present in both snapshots via includeInactive). Node.active get cross-confirms.
specs.expect: step1 false; call result.changed.activated includes ref:'Canvas/Panel'; step3 true.

specs.title: call a pure getter (getCount) equals get of the same field — two copse paths concur, no mutation
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(4).
specs.groundTruth: getCount(){return this.count;} returns the field value; call (callMethod) and get (readProp) are independent copse code paths over the same component instance and must agree. Deterministic predicted value 4 anchors both (not a copse-said-X-copse-said-X loop: the 4 came from setCount, the assertion is mutual agreement on the predicted constant).
specs.expect: call value===4 AND get value===4 (equal each other and the predicted 4); no mutation (count still 4).

specs.title: call returning an object (getState) serializes faithfully through the MCP boundary
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(3), gold 70.
specs.groundTruth: getState(){return {count:this.count,gold:this.gold};} -> the structured value must round-trip page.evaluate -> JSON -> MCP. Deterministic predicted {count:3,gold:70} (gold per fixture default after prior buy specs reset).
specs.expect: call -> {ok:true, value:{count:3, gold:<predicted>}} — object value preserved (not [object Object]/null).

specs.title: happy-path press result shape: omit-default fields (no wired/touched on a clean clickEvent press)
specs.setup: Connected; setCount(0).
specs.groundTruth: core press: on a fired>0 clickEvent press, drove=['clickEvent'] (array, not the 'nothing' string), and `wired`/`touched` are NOT added (the !drove.length||includes('touch') branch is false). Contract assertion (no external oracle).
specs.expect: press keys are exactly {ok,ref,fired,drove}(+optional changed); drove===['clickEvent'] (array); 'wired' not in result; 'touched' not in result.

specs.title: runHarness hard-fails a plan that presses a drove:'nothing' button (driveGate)
specs.setup: Live preview. Node script: const cp=await connect('http://localhost:7456'); a deterministic Agent whose plan returns steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}] and judge returns {pass:true}; runHarness(cp, agent).
specs.action: node -e "import('copse/driver-puppeteer').then(async({connect})=>{const {runHarness}=await import('copse/harness'); const cp=await connect('http://localhost:7456'); const agent={plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}]}),judge:()=>({pass:true})}; console.log(JSON.stringify(await runHarness(cp,agent))); await cp.close();})"
specs.groundTruth: DeadBtn is authored with no clickEvents/no code handlers (deterministic) -> the REAL press returns drove:'nothing'; runHarness driveGate collects it into out.undriven and forces out.pass=false EVEN THOUGH judge said pass:true. coir independently confirms DeadBtn has zero ClickEvent edges.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.undriven===[{ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}]; the pass:true judge is overridden by the gate.

specs.title: runHarness hard-fails a plan that presses a throwing handler (errorGate)
specs.setup: Live preview. Agent plan presses Canvas/ThrowBtn; judge {pass:true}.
specs.groundTruth: ThrowBtn -> boom() throws 'copse-test-boom'; the driver attaches result.errors (CDP console capture). runHarness errorGate collects it into out.errored and forces out.pass=false, overriding the passing judge. Deterministic message is the oracle.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.errored contains {ref:'Canvas/ThrowBtn', error:<text incl 'copse-test-boom'>}.

specs.title: runHarness hard-fails a press to a covered button (reachableGate) even though press succeeds
specs.setup: Live preview. Canvas/CoveredBtn under full-screen Canvas/Overlay (BlockInputEvents). Agent plan presses CoveredBtn; judge {pass:true}.
specs.groundTruth: copse press CALLS the handler (count would increment), but reachable() reports reachable:false/blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay' (geometric z-order), so execStep tags result.unreachable and runHarness forces out.pass=false. Independent oracle: mcp__copse__reachable {ref:'Canvas/CoveredBtn'} -> reachable:false/blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay'; coir confirms Overlay is a later-drawn sibling over CoveredBtn.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.unreachable includes {ref:'Canvas/CoveredBtn', blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay'} (a press copse could make but a player could not is not a pass).

specs.title: runHarness passes a clean clickEvent press whose judge expects the predicted count delta
specs.setup: Live preview. Agent: plan presses AddBtn after setCount(0); judge reads count via a get step and returns pass = (count===1).
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic: a real clickEvent press drives count 0->1; no gate fires (drove:['clickEvent'], no errors, reachable). out.pass true reflects the genuine delta. The judge's oracle value 1 comes from the fixture source.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.undriven/out.errored/out.unreachable all absent; the press step result fired:1/drove:['clickEvent'].

specs.title: single-shot `copse press` reports the delta via `changed` within one session (state does not survive across CLI invocations)
specs.setup: Live preview; AddBtn wired, ScoreLabel bound.
specs.groundTruth: Each single-shot CLI command connects -> one primitive -> close, so a mutation does NOT persist to a separate `copse get` invocation (fresh browser session). The delta is therefore visible only via the press's OWN `changed` block in the same run. Deterministic source: pressing once -> ScoreLabel '0'->'1' (assuming a freshly-loaded scene baseline 0). Oracle = the labelChanged from/to predicted by the fixture.
specs.expect: stdout JSON has {ok:true, fired:1, drove:['clickEvent'], changed:{labelChanged:[{ref:'Canvas/ScoreLabel', from:'0', to:'1'}], ...}}; a follow-up separate `copse get` would read a fresh baseline, NOT 1 (documents the per-invocation session boundary).

specs.title: copse preview press and editor edit-mode call produce the IDENTICAL deterministic count delta (0->1)
specs.setup: Fixture saved. Two runtimes: the preview (copse) and the editor edit-mode scene.
specs.action: EDITOR: 1) mcp__cocos-creator__component_get_component_info {node:'Canvas/Mgr', component:'CopseCounter'} -> count A0  2) mcp__cocos-creator__sceneAdvanced_execute_component_method {node:'Canvas/Mgr', component:'CopseCounter', method:'add', args:[]}  3) component_get_component_info -> count A1.  PREVIEW: 4) mcp__copse__call setCount [A0]  5) mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/AddBtn"}  6) mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count"} -> count P1
specs.groundTruth: Both runtimes execute the SAME authored add() source. The editor's direct method call (A1-A0) and copse's wired-clickEvent press (P1-A0) must yield the identical +1 delta. If copse's press did not actuate add(), P1 would not move while A1 does -> divergence flags a press bug. This is a genuine cross-runtime/cross-tool oracle (editor MCP vs copse) for the same deterministic operation.
specs.expect: A1-A0===1 (editor) AND P1-A0===1 (copse) — the wired press reproduces the canonical handler delta in an independent runtime.

specs.title: copse reads the authored zero baseline that the editor independently reports (anchors every delta to a tool-agreed start)
specs.setup: Fixture freshly authored+saved; copse connected to a freshly-loaded preview (no presses yet).
specs.groundTruth: The preview is built from the editor's saved scene, so copse's fresh readback of authored fields must equal the editor's edit-mode readback (independent tool, the source of those values). count authored 0; ScoreLabel.string authored '0'. This is the non-circular anchor: delta specs measure FROM a baseline that copse and the editor agree on.
specs.expect: copse count===0 and editor CopseCounter.count===0; copse Label.string==='0' and editor ScoreLabel Label.string==='0' (all four agree).

specs.title: emitTouch is NOT synthesized when a code on('click') handler exists (no double-actuation)
specs.setup: Connected; codeCount(0). Canvas/CodeClickBtn (on('click') only, no clickEvents).
specs.groundTruth: core press: fired===0 BUT droveClick===true, so the `fired===0 && !droveClick` guard blocks emitTouch -> touched stays false and codeCount increments by EXACTLY 1 (click only), not 2 (click+touch). Deterministic oracle: codeCount=1.
specs.expect: press result has NO `touched` key (and drove===['click']); codeCount===1 (a synthesized touch would make it 2 if a touch handler also existed — here it proves no extra emitTouch).

specs.title: A button with nothing over it is reachable:true, visible:true, no blocker/occluder
specs.setup: Author ReachScene per fixturesNeeded; reload preview to it; mcp__copse__connect to the preview URL.
specs.groundTruth: Authored geometry: AddBtn sits at (0,240) with NO later-sibling overlay/sprite covering its center (verify via cocos-creator node_get_node_info on every Canvas child — no node positioned over (0,240)). Independent of copse.
specs.expect: {ok:true, reachable:true, blockedBy:null, visible:true} and the response has NO occludedBy key.

specs.title: A BlockInputEvents overlay drawn on top makes a button reachable:false with blockedBy naming it
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded; Overlay (BlockInputEvents) is a LATER sibling than CoveredBtn, sized 200x80 at (0,120).
specs.groundTruth: Authored overlay geometry, corroborated independently: (a) coir/cocos-creator confirms Canvas/Overlay carries cc.BlockInputEvents and its child index > CoveredBtn's; (b) cocos-creator confirms Overlay's UITransform box contains CoveredBtn's center (0,120). copse never read back to derive this.
specs.expect: reachable===false AND blockedBy==="Canvas/Overlay".

specs.title: The input-consumer set includes cc.Button (a button over a button blocks it)
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded; OverlayBtn (a cc.Button) is a later sibling over CoveredBtn2 at (0,-240).
specs.groundTruth: Authored geometry: cocos-creator confirms OverlayBtn has cc.Button, sits later in sibling order than CoveredBtn2, and its box covers (0,-240). Proves a Button (not only BlockInputEvents) is treated as a touch consumer.
specs.expect: reachable===false AND blockedBy==="Canvas/OverlayBtn".

specs.title: An opaque non-consumer sprite over a button → reachable:true but occludedBy set (the headline honest boundary)
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded; Banner (Sprite only, opacity 255, NO Button/BIE) is a later sibling over OccludedBtn at (0,0).
specs.groundTruth: Authored geometry: cocos-creator confirms Banner has ONLY cc.Sprite (no Button/BlockInputEvents), opacity 255, later sibling than OccludedBtn, box covers (0,0). Touch ignores opacity so reach is true; the sprite hides it visually. Oracle is the authored layering, not copse.
specs.expect: reachable===true AND occludedBy==="Canvas/Banner" AND blockedBy===null.

specs.title: A renderer-only node occludes but never blocks (it is not an input consumer)
specs.setup: Same OccludedBtn/Banner fixture.
specs.groundTruth: Authored: Banner has no Button/BlockInputEvents (cocos-creator component_get_components on Canvas/Banner lists only Sprite+UITransform). The walk's `consumer = Button||BlockInputEvents` is therefore null for Banner, so it can be `occ` but never `top`.
specs.expect: blockedBy===null (Banner is NOT reported as a blocker) while occludedBy==="Canvas/Banner" — the two signals are kept distinct.

specs.title: A button's own descendant sprite is excluded from occlusion (isAncestor guard)
specs.setup: SelfBgBtn with an opaque child Sprite Bg covering it, nothing else over it.
specs.groundTruth: Authored parent-child relationship: cocos-creator confirms Bg is a CHILD of SelfBgBtn. The occluder branch requires `!isAncestor(n,x) && !isAncestor(x,n)`, so a descendant cannot occlude its own button.
specs.expect: reachable===true AND the response has NO occludedBy key (own background not mistaken for an occluder).

specs.title: An opaque sprite invisible via UIOpacity:0 does not occlude (visibleOf gate on the occluder)
specs.setup: InvisOccBtn under InvisBanner (Sprite, later sibling, UIOpacity.opacity=0) at (0,-120).
specs.groundTruth: Authored: cocos-creator confirms InvisBanner.UIOpacity.opacity===0. The occluder branch requires `visibleOf(x)` true; an opacity-0 sprite fails it, so it is not counted as an occluder.
specs.expect: reachable===true AND NO occludedBy key (an invisible sprite is not an occluder).

specs.title: UIOpacity:0 on the button sets visible:false while keeping reachable:true (separate signals)
specs.setup: OpacityBtn with its own cc.UIOpacity.opacity=0 at (0,300), nothing over it.
specs.groundTruth: Independent oracle: cocos-creator component_get_component_info reads Canvas/OpacityBtn UIOpacity.opacity===0 (the authored value). visibleOf is opacity===0 → false; input ignores opacity → reach unaffected.
specs.expect: reachable===true AND visible===false (combine reachable && visible ⇒ false: present-to-touch, invisible-to-eye).

specs.title: Ancestor UIOpacity:0 propagates visible:false to a child button (visibleOf walks the chain)
specs.setup: HiddenPanel.UIOpacity.opacity=0 containing PanelBtn (own opacity 255).
specs.groundTruth: Authored: cocos-creator reads HiddenPanel UIOpacity.opacity===0 and PanelBtn has NO UIOpacity (own opacity 255). visibleOf ascends parents so the panel's 0 dominates.
specs.expect: visible===false even though the button's own opacity is 255.

specs.title: Ancestor scale (0,0) sets visible:false (the scale branch of visibleOf)
specs.setup: ScalePanel node scale set to (0,0) containing ScaleBtn.
specs.groundTruth: Authored: cocos-creator node_get_node_info reads ScalePanel scale.x===0 (and y===0). visibleOf returns false on `s.x===0 || s.y===0` anywhere up the chain. (reachable may be 'unsure' as the world box collapses — assert visible only.)
specs.expect: visible===false (regardless of the reachable tri-state value).

specs.title: A button in an active:false panel is reachable:'unsure' (can't judge → fail loud, not true)
specs.setup: ClosedPanel.active=false containing ClosedBtn.
specs.groundTruth: Authored: cocos-creator reads ClosedPanel.active===false → ClosedBtn.activeInHierarchy false. The geometric walk only considers activeInHierarchy nodes, so nothing (not even the button) hits its own center → top stays null → 'unsure'. The unrendered state is the authored oracle.
specs.expect: reachable==="unsure" (the literal string, NOT true and NOT false) — uncertainty fails loud.

specs.title: reachable:'unsure' never names a blocker; reachable:false ALWAYS does
specs.setup: Use the ClosedBtn ('unsure') and CoveredBtn (false) results.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (src/core/index.js reachable + src/cocos/runtime.js: 'unsure' branches return blockedBy:null; the false branch always sets blockedBy=refOf(top)). No external oracle — structural invariant.
specs.expect: For ClosedBtn: blockedBy===null. For CoveredBtn: blockedBy is a non-null/non-empty ref string. (reachable:false ⇒ a named blocker always.)

specs.title: reachable on a non-existent ref returns ok:false reason:not-found (no geometry attempted)
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (src/core/index.js reachable: resolve→null ⇒ {ok:false, ref, reason:'not-found'}). No node authored at that ref.
specs.expect: {ok:false, ref:"Canvas/GhostBtn", reason:"not-found"} and NO reachable/visible keys.

specs.title: core reachable returns reason:'unsupported' when the Runtime has no reachable()
specs.setup: Node unit test over a fake Runtime that omits the optional reachable method.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core line: `if (!rt.reachable) return {ok:false, reason:'unsupported'}`). Deterministic over the fake tree — the optional-adapter contract.
specs.expect: {ok:false, ref:'Canvas/AddBtn', reason:'unsupported'}.

specs.title: core reachable defaults visible:true when the Runtime omits the visible field
specs.setup: Fake Runtime whose reachable returns {reachable:true} only.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core: `visible: r.visible ?? true`). Deterministic over the fake — pins the visible default so a minimal adapter still yields a usable shape.
specs.expect: {ok:true, ref:'Canvas/AddBtn', reachable:true, blockedBy:null, visible:true}.

specs.title: interactive() annotates a blocked button with reachable:false + blockedBy
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded; CoveredBtn is active and covered by Overlay.
specs.groundTruth: Authored overlay geometry (same oracle as REACH-blocked-by-BIE: Overlay has BlockInputEvents, later sibling, covers (0,120)). interactive() must carry the per-button reachability the standalone reachable() reports.
specs.expect: The row with ref==="Canvas/CoveredBtn" has reachable===false AND blockedBy==="Canvas/Overlay".

specs.title: interactive() omits inactive buttons (reachability ride-along only covers active buttons)
specs.setup: ClosedPanel.active=false containing ClosedBtn.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (snapshot onlyInteractive defaults includeInactive:false ⇒ inactive subtrees skipped) + authored ClosedPanel.active===false (cocos-creator readback). Implication under test: to judge a hidden button's reachability you must call reachable() directly.
specs.expect: NO row with ref==="Canvas/ClosedPanel/ClosedBtn" appears in the interactive() output.

specs.title: Plain snapshot omits reachable; interactive (reachability:true) includes it
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core snapshot reachability default false; install/tools wires interactive→reachability:true). No external oracle — the opt-in contract that keeps the O(buttons×nodes) pass off the cheap path.
specs.expect: In snapshot() output NO button descriptor has a `reachable` key; in interactive() output every button descriptor has a `reachable` key.

specs.title: interactive() emits blockedBy only on reachable:false rows, never on reachable:true
specs.setup: ReachScene: AddBtn(true) and CoveredBtn(false) both active.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core: `if (r.reachable === false && r.blockedBy) desc.blockedBy = r.blockedBy`) plus authored geometry (AddBtn unobstructed, CoveredBtn covered).
specs.expect: AddBtn descriptor has NO blockedBy key; CoveredBtn descriptor has blockedBy==="Canvas/Overlay".

specs.title: interactive() emits visible only when false (true is omitted)
specs.setup: ReachScene: AddBtn(visible) and OpacityBtn(opacity 0) both active.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core: `if (r.visible === false) desc.visible = false`) plus authored OpacityBtn.UIOpacity.opacity===0 (cocos-creator readback).
specs.expect: AddBtn descriptor has NO visible key; OpacityBtn descriptor has visible===false.

specs.title: interactive() emits occludedBy whenever present, even on reachable:true rows
specs.setup: ReachScene: OccludedBtn under Banner.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (core: `if (r.occludedBy) desc.occludedBy = r.occludedBy` — unconditional on reachable) plus authored Banner layering.
specs.expect: OccludedBtn descriptor has reachable===true AND occludedBy==="Canvas/Banner".

specs.title: click_surface rows carry reachable/blockedBy/occludedBy/visible for the coir join
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded; CoveredBtn, OpacityBtn, OccludedBtn all have an editor-wired clickEvent so they emit join rows.
specs.groundTruth: Authored geometry (CoveredBtn blocked, OpacityBtn opacity 0, OccludedBtn under Banner). These ride-along flags are exactly what coverageJoin buckets into blocked/uncertain — cross-referenced against coir's static (nodePath,method) map for the same scene.
specs.expect: The CoveredBtn row has reachable:false+blockedBy:"Canvas/Overlay"; the OpacityBtn row has visible:false; the OccludedBtn row has reachable:true+occludedBy:"Canvas/Banner".

specs.title: click_surface(reachability:false) strips all reach/visibility flags
specs.setup: ReachScene loaded.
specs.groundTruth: copse contract (install clickSurface: reachability !== false default true; passing false runs snapshot without the reachable pass, so clickSurface has nothing to ride along).
specs.expect: No row in the output has any of: reachable, blockedBy, occludedBy, visible.

specs.title: runHarness HARD-FAILS a press to a reachable:false button (overrides judge/report)
specs.setup: Library test: a Driver whose reachable(ref) returns {reachable:false, blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay'} and whose press returns {ok:true,fired:1,drove:['clickEvent']}; an Agent that plans press 'Canvas/CoveredBtn' and a judge that returns pass:true.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract over the fixture: the gate collects unreachablePresses and forces out.pass=false (src/harness.js lines ~91/99/158/185). A passing judge must not override a covered press.
specs.expect: out.pass===false AND out.unreachable contains {ref:'Canvas/CoveredBtn', blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay'} AND the step result carries unreachable:'Canvas/Overlay'.

specs.title: runHarness SURFACES (not hard-fail) a press to an occludedBy button as out.uncertain
specs.setup: Driver.reachable returns {reachable:true, occludedBy:'Canvas/Banner'}; press returns ok; judge returns pass:true.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract (src/harness.js: occludedBy ⇒ uncertain='occluded:Canvas/Banner', surfaced as out.uncertain but NOT in the pass gate). occludedBy is a verify signal, not a hard fail.
specs.expect: out.uncertain contains {ref:'Canvas/...', why:'occluded:Canvas/Banner'} AND out.pass is NOT forced false by the reach gate (no out.unreachable).

specs.title: runHarness surfaces a press to a reachable:'unsure' button as out.uncertain why:'unsure'
specs.setup: Driver.reachable returns {reachable:'unsure', blockedBy:null}; press returns ok; judge pass:true.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract (src/harness.js: reachable==='unsure' ⇒ uncertain='unsure', surfaced not hard-failed).
specs.expect: out.uncertain contains {ref, why:'unsure'} AND there is no out.unreachable entry for it.

specs.title: press {force:true} bypasses the reachability gate (no out.unreachable)
specs.setup: Driver.reachable returns {reachable:false, blockedBy:'X'}; the planned step is {op:'press', ref:'Canvas/CoveredBtn', opts:{force:true}}.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract (src/harness.js: `!(step.opts && step.opts.force)` gates the check — force skips it entirely, never calling reachable).
specs.expect: out has no `unreachable` array AND the reach gate does not force pass=false (verdict decided by judge/report only).

specs.title: reachableGate:false disables the gate even for a covered press
specs.setup: Same covered driver as REACH-harness-gate-unreachable; judge pass:true.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract (src/harness.js: reachableGate option default true; false skips the reachable() call and the hard fail). Mirrors test/harness.test.js:153.
specs.expect: out.pass===true AND out has no `unreachable` field.

specs.title: runHarness degrades gracefully when the Driver has no reachable() (gate is a no-op)
specs.setup: Driver WITHOUT a reachable method; press returns ok; judge pass:true.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic harness contract (src/harness.js: `typeof driver.reachable === 'function'` guards the gate — absent ⇒ skip, today's behavior).
specs.expect: out.pass===true AND no `unreachable`/`uncertain` from the reach gate (the gate silently no-ops).

specs.title: copse CLI single-shot reachable returns the blocked verdict as JSON
specs.setup: Preview serving ReachScene at the rig URL.
specs.groundTruth: Authored overlay geometry (Overlay=BlockInputEvents later sibling over CoveredBtn), corroborated by cocos-creator readback — same independent oracle as REACH-blocked-by-BIE, exercised through the connect→one-primitive→JSON→close CLI path.
specs.expect: stdout is JSON with reachable===false AND blockedBy==="Canvas/Overlay" (and ok:true).

specs.title: With stacked input-consumers, blockedBy names the TOP-most (highest orderKey)
specs.setup: (Advanced) StackBtn under Mask1(below) and Mask2(above) — both BlockInputEvents, Mask2 a later sibling.
specs.groundTruth: Authored sibling order: cocos-creator confirms Mask2's child index > Mask1's > StackBtn's, both covering (0,-300). The walk keeps the consumer with the greatest orderKey via cmpKey.
specs.expect: reachable===false AND blockedBy==="Canvas/Mask2" (the upper overlay, not Mask1).

specs.title: occludedBy and blockedBy are independent: a button can be reachable:true+occludedBy while a sibling is reachable:false+blockedBy
specs.setup: ReachScene: OccludedBtn (sprite over it) and CoveredBtn (BIE over it) both active.
specs.groundTruth: Authored layering: Banner is renderer-only (occludes), Overlay is an input-consumer (blocks) — distinct authored component sets per cocos-creator readback. Confirms the two signals never collapse into one.
specs.expect: OccludedBtn: reachable:true, occludedBy set, NO blockedBy. CoveredBtn: reachable:false, blockedBy set, NO occludedBy.

specs.title: Closed panel: copse reads back the editor-authored Panel.active=false
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded in preview, copse connected, no action taken (initial state).
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator node_get_node_info on Canvas/Panel reports active===false (the value WE authored in the editor); this is read before any preview mutation so editor and preview agree on the initial state.
specs.expect: snapshot(includeInactive) contains Canvas/Panel (and its labeled children) with active:false; node('Canvas/Panel') => {active:false, activeInHierarchy:false}; get('Canvas/Panel:Node.active').value===false — all matching the editor-authored false.

specs.title: Default snapshot omits the entire closed-panel subtree (recursion guard)
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed (initial).
specs.action: mcp__copse__snapshot {} (relevant default true, includeInactive false) vs mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true}
specs.groundTruth: Source contract: snapshot() returns before visiting children of an inactive node, so an inactive Panel hides its whole subtree; the panel IS authored inactive (editor node_get_node_info Panel.active===false).
specs.expect: Default snapshot contains NONE of Canvas/Panel/Title, Canvas/Panel/CountLabel, Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn; the includeInactive snapshot contains all three. Difference is exactly the panel subtree.

specs.title: Open via call: panel subtree flips into diff.activated (not appeared)
specs.surface: core diff over snapshot({includeInactive:true}) before/after; MCP call+diff
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic PanelController.open() (authored by us) sets panel.active=true => predicted activeInHierarchy flip false->true for the panel subtree; corroborated by copse node('Canvas/Panel') (separate nodeInfo code path, not the diff machinery) reading activeInHierarchy:true after.
specs.expect: diff.activated includes Canvas/Panel/Title, Canvas/Panel/CountLabel, Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn (active flipped false->true); none of them appear in diff.appeared; diff.deactivated empty.

specs.title: Press OpenBtn auto-attaches changed.activated for the panel subtree
specs.surface: puppeteer mutate()->changed (settle + snapDiff includeInactive); MCP press
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.groundTruth: OpenBtn's wired clickEvent -> PanelController.open() (deterministic; coir static ClickEvent confirms OpenBtn click->open). press returns fired>=1 and drove includes 'clickEvent'; the panel state change is the known open() effect.
specs.expect: result.fired>=1, result.drove includes 'clickEvent' (not 'nothing'); result.changed.activated includes Canvas/Panel/Title and Canvas/Panel/CountLabel; result has no error field.

specs.title: Close: panel subtree flips into diff.deactivated
specs.surface: core diff over includeInactive snapshots; MCP press/call + diff
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel OPEN (run open first).
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__press {"ref":"Canvas/CloseBtn"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic PanelController.close() sets panel.active=false => predicted activeInHierarchy true->false; corroborated by copse node('Canvas/Panel').activeInHierarchy:false after (independent nodeInfo path). press.changed.deactivated should also carry it.
specs.expect: diff (and press.changed) deactivated includes Canvas/Panel/Title and Canvas/Panel/CountLabel; activated empty; node('Canvas/Panel').active===false.

specs.title: includeInactive is what turns 'appeared' into 'activated' for a panel
specs.surface: core diff + snapshot includeInactive vs default
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed; run the SAME open() between the two before-captures.
specs.action: beforeDefault = mcp__copse__snapshot {} ; beforeAll = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; diffA = mcp__copse__diff {"before":beforeDefault,"after":after} ; diffB = mcp__copse__diff {"before":beforeAll,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: Documented diff() semantics (core/index.js:299-303): a ref absent from `before` is `appeared`; a ref present in both with an active flip is `activated`. A default before-snapshot omits the inactive subtree, an includeInactive one keeps it.
specs.expect: diffA.appeared includes Canvas/Panel/Title (it was absent from the active-only before) and diffA.activated does NOT; diffB.activated includes Canvas/Panel/Title and diffB.appeared does NOT. Same mutation, classification differs purely by includeInactive.

specs.title: Close classified as deactivated (includeInactive after) vs disappeared (default after)
specs.surface: core diff + snapshot includeInactive vs default
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel OPEN.
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.close"} ; afterDefault = mcp__copse__snapshot {} ; afterAll = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; diffA = mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":afterDefault} ; diffB = mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":afterAll}
specs.groundTruth: diff() contract: a ref dropped from `after` is `disappeared`; present-in-both with active true->false is `deactivated`. The default after-snapshot drops the now-inactive subtree.
specs.expect: diffA.disappeared includes Canvas/Panel/Title; diffB.deactivated includes Canvas/Panel/Title and diffB.disappeared does NOT — same close, classification driven by includeInactive on the after-snapshot.

specs.title: Bound CountLabel surfaces in labelChanged with from/to matching the controller
specs.surface: core diff labelChanged; cross-checked via MCP get on the controller field
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed (openCount=0, CountLabel '0').
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; d = mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after} ; mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.openCount"} ; mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Panel/CountLabel:Label.string"}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic open() does openCount=0->1 and countLabel.string='1'. Independent oracle: get('Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.openCount').value (a component-field read, NOT the snapshot/diff path) must equal Number(labelChanged.to). Authored baseline openCount=0 read from editor component_get_component_info.
specs.expect: d.labelChanged contains {ref:'Canvas/Panel/CountLabel', from:'0', to:'1'}; get(...openCount).value===1; get('Canvas/Panel/CountLabel:Label.string').value==='1' — the diff's `to` agrees with the independent field read.

specs.title: Always-active StatusLabel: open shows it only in labelChanged, never activated
specs.surface: core diff — labelChanged vs activated separation
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed; StatusLabel ('idle') is at Canvas root, always active.
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: open() sets statusLabel.string='open' but never touches StatusLabel.active; deterministic. StatusLabel is authored always-active (editor node_get_node_info active===true, unchanged).
specs.expect: diff.labelChanged contains {ref:'Canvas/StatusLabel', from:'idle', to:'open'}; Canvas/StatusLabel appears in NEITHER activated NOR deactivated NOR appeared.

specs.title: diff treats omitted `active` as active (db.active !== false branch)
specs.surface: core diff — `const wasActive = db.active !== false, isActive = da.active !== false`
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded; capture two snapshots around an open() (StatusLabel active in both, so slim snapshot OMITS its `active` field in both).
specs.action: Inspect the same before/after snapshots from PANEL-labelChanged-not-activated: confirm neither StatusLabel descriptor carries an `active` key, then mcp__copse__diff on them.
specs.groundTruth: Source: slim snapshot omits active when true; diff coerces missing active to active. So a node active-in-both with no `active` field is correctly NOT classified activated/deactivated even though `active` is absent.
specs.expect: Both StatusLabel descriptors have no `active` property; diff.deactivated and diff.activated contain no Canvas/StatusLabel entry (only labelChanged). Pins the omitted-active = active rule.

specs.title: Opening flips activeInHierarchy for EVERY relevant descendant
specs.surface: core diff activated; cross-checked via MCP node() per child
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.action: open via mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; diff includeInactive before/after ; then mcp__copse__node for Canvas/Panel/Title, Canvas/Panel/CountLabel, Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn
specs.groundTruth: activeInHierarchy semantics: a child inherits its ancestor's inactive state, so flipping Panel.active=true flips activeInHierarchy for all descendants. Independent: node() on each child reports activeInHierarchy:true (nodeInfo path, not diff).
specs.expect: diff.activated includes all three labeled/button descendants of Panel; node() on each returns activeInHierarchy:true after open (and was false before).

specs.title: activated entries are full descriptors carrying the panel's contents
specs.surface: core diff — activated/appeared push `da` (the after-descriptor), not bare refs
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed; Title authored string 'Shop'.
specs.action: open then mcp__copse__diff includeInactive before/after; inspect the activated entries.
specs.groundTruth: Source: appeared/activated push the descriptor (ref + label/button/click). Title.string='Shop' and PanelCloseBtn is a Button are editor-authored (cocos-creator component_get_component_info).
specs.expect: The diff.activated entry for Canvas/Panel/Title has .label==='Shop'; the entry for Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn has .button===true (and a .click array) — opening hands you the panel's contents without a follow-up snapshot.

specs.title: node() intrinsics are the 'did the panel open' basis (active/activeInHierarchy)
specs.surface: core node()/MCP node + get Node pseudo-component
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.groundTruth: Editor-authored initial active:false (node_get_node_info) + deterministic open() => predicted active:true/activeInHierarchy:true. get via the Node pseudo-component is a third independent read path (readProp on the node).
specs.expect: node() before: {active:false, activeInHierarchy:false}; after: {active:true, activeInHierarchy:true}; get('Canvas/Panel:Node.active').value flips false->true. All three agree.

specs.title: Standalone diff tool over two manually-captured snapshots
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic open(); the diff tool is the same core diff() exercised independently of press.changed.
specs.expect: diff tool returns {appeared,disappeared,activated,deactivated,labelChanged}; activated includes the panel subtree; labelChanged includes Canvas/Panel/CountLabel and Canvas/StatusLabel — equivalent to the auto-attached press.changed.

specs.title: relevant:true drops the bare Panel container; relevant:false keeps it in activated
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed; Canvas/Panel has NO Label/Button on the container itself.
specs.action: beforeRel = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true,"relevant":true} ; beforeAll = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true,"relevant":false} ; open via mcp__copse__call ; capture after both ways ; mcp__copse__diff for each pair
specs.groundTruth: Source: relevant keeps only button|label|codeHandler nodes; a bare container is filtered. The Panel container is authored with no Label/Button (editor component_get_components shows none).
specs.expect: relevant diff.activated does NOT contain the bare 'Canvas/Panel' ref (only its labeled children); relevant:false diff.activated DOES contain 'Canvas/Panel' itself (active false->true). The driver's press.changed (relevant:true) therefore reports children, not the container.

specs.title: A press that drives but changes nothing attaches NO `changed`
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed; NoopBtn wired to PanelController.noop (does nothing).
specs.groundTruth: noop() mutates no node/label (deterministic), so all five diff arrays are empty => the mutate() guard omits `changed`. drove is still ['clickEvent'] because the click fired.
specs.expect: result.drove includes 'clickEvent' (the press actuated) but result has NO `changed` key and no `errors`; distinguishes 'drove something' from 'changed the panel'.

specs.title: diff of two identical snapshots is fully empty
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, any stable state, no action between captures.
specs.groundTruth: diff() contract: identical inputs => every bucket empty (no external oracle needed; pure function over equal arrays).
specs.expect: diff => {appeared:[], disappeared:[], activated:[], deactivated:[], labelChanged:[]} (modulo any live ticking Label, which would only ever land in labelChanged).

specs.title: Re-open an already-open panel: labelChanged increments, NOT re-activated
specs.surface: core diff — activated requires a false->true flip
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded; run open() once (panel active, CountLabel '1').
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.open"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":after} ; mcp__copse__get {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.openCount"}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic open() on an already-open panel: active stays true (no flip) but openCount 1->2, countLabel '1'->'2'. Independent: get(openCount).value===2.
specs.expect: diff.activated is empty (panel was already active); diff.labelChanged contains {ref:'Canvas/Panel/CountLabel', from:'1', to:'2'}; get(openCount).value===2 — idempotency-of-active with a real counter delta.

specs.title: toggle() alternates activated/deactivated across calls
specs.surface: core diff over repeated MCP call + snapshot
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
