specs.action: Loop 3x: before=snapshot includeInactive ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.toggle"} ; after=snapshot includeInactive ; mcp__copse__diff
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic toggle(): closed->open->closed->open. Predicted: round1 activated, round2 deactivated, round3 activated. Cross-checked by node('Canvas/Panel').active after each toggle.
specs.expect: Round 1 & 3 diffs put the panel subtree in activated; round 2 puts it in deactivated; node('Canvas/Panel').active reads true,false,true respectively — a clean two-state machine.

specs.title: Dynamically added/destroyed node => appeared / disappeared (node existence)
specs.surface: core diff — appeared (ref only in after) / disappeared (ref only in before)
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded; no Toast present.
specs.action: before = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.spawn"} ; mid = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; diff1 = mcp__copse__diff {"before":before,"after":mid} ; mcp__copse__call {"sel":"Canvas/Ctrl:PanelController.despawn"} ; after = mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; diff2 = mcp__copse__diff {"before":mid,"after":after}
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic spawn() addChild a Node('Toast') with a Label 'Saved' under Canvas; despawn() destroys it. Node-existence change (not an active flip) => appeared then disappeared.
specs.expect: diff1.appeared contains Canvas/Toast (descriptor with label 'Saved'); diff1.activated empty; diff2.disappeared contains Canvas/Toast; diff2.deactivated empty — appeared/disappeared distinct from activated/deactivated.

specs.title: labelChanged entries are {ref,from,to}, distinct from descriptor buckets
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded; produce a labelChanged via open().
specs.action: Use the diff from PANEL-labelChanged-bound; assert the shape of labelChanged vs appeared/activated.
specs.groundTruth: Source: labelChanged carries {ref, from, to} (from/to via `?? null`), whereas appeared/activated/disappeared/deactivated carry node descriptors. Structural contract.
specs.expect: Each labelChanged entry has exactly keys ref/from/to (no button/label/click); appeared/activated entries are descriptors (have ref + label/button). Confirms the two distinct shapes.

specs.title: copse's panel subtree agrees with coir (static) and the editor hierarchy
specs.surface: core snapshot includeInactive vs coir tree vs cocos-creator hierarchy
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded in editor + preview.
specs.action: mcp__copse__snapshot {"includeInactive":true} ; mcp__coir__tree {scene: PanelScene} (or mcp__coir__find for Panel) ; mcp__cocos-creator__scene_get_scene_hierarchy
specs.groundTruth: coir's STATIC node graph and cocos-creator's editor hierarchy independently list Panel's children (Title, CountLabel, PanelCloseBtn) under Canvas/Panel — two non-copse oracles for the tree copse walks.
specs.expect: copse refs Canvas/Panel/Title, Canvas/Panel/CountLabel, Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn (and the OpenBtn/CloseBtn/StatusLabel siblings) match coir's nodePaths and the editor hierarchy (modulo coir's scene-root prefix).

specs.title: copse click_surface for OpenBtn matches coir's static ClickEvent (open)
specs.surface: core clickSurface / MCP click_surface vs coir ClickEvent map
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded.
specs.action: mcp__copse__click_surface {} ; mcp__coir__find {what: ClickEvents on OpenBtn} (or mcp__coir__tree showing the click->open edge)
specs.groundTruth: coir statically reads OpenBtn.clickEvents -> handler 'open' on PanelController; copse reads the same wiring live. The (ref, method) join key must agree across the two independent tools.
specs.expect: copse click_surface has a row {ref:'Canvas/OpenBtn', method:'open'} (and CloseBtn->close, PanelCloseBtn->close); coir reports the identical click->open edge for OpenBtn — the wired handler that drives the panel is confirmed by both.

specs.title: PanelCloseBtn is only reachable after the panel opens (best-effort)
specs.surface: core reachable / MCP reachable across the open transition
specs.setup: PanelScene loaded, panel closed.
specs.groundTruth: Authored initial Panel.active=false makes PanelCloseBtn.activeInHierarchy=false, so it is not an input consumer at its center (geometric heuristic, best-effort). After open() its activeInHierarchy=true and (no overlay above) it should be on top. Honest: reachable is tri-state and best-effort; 'unsure'/false closed is acceptable, true open is the signal.
specs.expect: Closed: reachable('Canvas/Panel/PanelCloseBtn') is false or 'unsure' (button inside an inactive panel can't be reached). Open: reachable===true with blockedBy:null. Treat as a verify signal, not gospel.

specs.title: CLI single-shot reads the authored closed-panel state
specs.setup: PanelScene served by the preview; each CLI invocation connects fresh (its own browser/runtime).
specs.action: copse node <preview-url> Canvas/Panel ; copse get <preview-url> Canvas/Panel:Node.active
specs.groundTruth: Editor-authored Panel.active=false; a fresh CLI connect loads the panel closed. NOTE: single-shot CLI is STATELESS per invocation (connect->op->close), so it can only read the initial/authored state, not a press effect from a prior invocation.
specs.expect: `copse node ... Canvas/Panel` JSON => active:false, activeInHierarchy:false; `copse get ... Canvas/Panel:Node.active` => value:false — matching the editor-authored false. (A press in a separate invocation would NOT persist.)

specs.title: diff tolerates empty/null snapshot inputs
specs.setup: No live state required (pure-function contract).
specs.groundTruth: Source guards map over `before||[]` / `after||[]`, so empty or null inputs produce empty buckets rather than throwing. Pure contract.
specs.expect: diff([],[]) and diff(null,null) both return {appeared:[],disappeared:[],activated:[],deactivated:[],labelChanged:[]} without error.

specs.title: listeners() surfaces a code-registered on('click') with its type/fn/target
specs.setup: Connect to the CodeScene preview (open CodeScene in the editor, copse reload). ClickCodeBtn's start() wired this.node.on('click', this.onClick, this).
specs.groundTruth: The authored fixture script source CopseClickHandler.start(): exactly one user listener, type 'click', callback method onClick, target class CopseClickHandler. (Code-registered node.on() is invisible to coir/cocos-creator, so the script source is the only authoring oracle.)
specs.expect: Returns exactly one entry: { type:'click', fn:'onClick', target:'CopseClickHandler' } (fn/target names present because the preview is a non-minified dev build — see code-minification-caveat).

specs.title: listeners() on a Button keeps the script's touch-end and DROPS the Button's own touch-* listeners
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. TouchCodeBtn has a cc.Button (which internally registers touch-start/move/end/cancel with target===the Button) plus CopseTouchHandler.start() wiring this.node.on(TOUCH_END, this.onTouch, this) (target===the script).
specs.groundTruth: Script source: one user touch-end on CopseTouchHandler. cc.Button's own four touch listeners are engine-internal (target identity === the Button component) and must be filtered per the documented codeHandlers contract.
specs.expect: Returns exactly [{ type:'touch-end', fn:'onTouch', target:'CopseTouchHandler' }]. NO entry with target:'Button'; NO touch-start/touch-move/touch-cancel entries (those exist on the node but belong to the Button → filtered).

specs.title: listeners() reports multiple distinct code handlers (click + touch-start) on one node
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. MultiCodeBtn.start() wired on('click', onClick) and on(TOUCH_START, onDown).
specs.groundTruth: Script source CopseMultiHandler: two user listeners — click→onClick and touch-start→onDown. (The Button's own touch-start with target===btn must be filtered, so the only touch-start returned is the script's.)
specs.expect: Returns exactly two entries (order-independent): { type:'click', fn:'onClick', target:'CopseMultiHandler' } and { type:'touch-start', fn:'onDown', target:'CopseMultiHandler' }; no duplicate/Button-owned touch-start.

specs.title: listeners() filters ENGINE_EVENTS and mouse-* even when user code wired them
specs.surface: src/cocos/runtime.js codeHandlers (ENGINE_EVENTS set + 'mouse-' prefix drop); mcp__copse__listeners
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. EngineEvtNode.start() wired this.node.on('transform-changed', ...) and this.node.on('mouse-down', ...).
specs.groundTruth: The fixture script wired BOTH events, but the documented codeHandlers contract drops 'transform-changed' (in ENGINE_EVENTS) and anything with a 'mouse-' prefix as engine/structural noise.
specs.expect: Returns [] (or, at most, entries for neither 'transform-changed' nor 'mouse-down') — both wired events are filtered out despite being user-registered.

specs.title: listeners() surfaces a code touch handler on a NON-button node (input-swallowing mask)
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. Overlay is a plain Node with CopseMaskHandler.start() wiring on(TOUCH_START, onSwallow); it has NO Button.
specs.groundTruth: Script source CopseMaskHandler: one user touch-start on a non-button node. codeHandlers reads the NodeEventProcessor directly and is not gated on the presence of a Button.
specs.expect: Returns [{ type:'touch-start', fn:'onSwallow', target:'CopseMaskHandler' }] (no Button present, so no Button-owned listeners to filter).

specs.title: listeners() returns [] for a button with no code-registered handlers
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. DeadBtn is a bare cc.Button (Button's own touch listeners only, all target===btn) with no script.
specs.groundTruth: Fixture: DeadBtn has no user script. The only listeners on it are the Button's own touch-* (target===btn), which the codeHandlers contract filters → nothing user-registered remains.
specs.expect: Returns [] (empty array, not null).

specs.title: listeners() on an unresolvable ref returns null (resolve miss)
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene.
specs.groundTruth: install.listeners does `const n = resolve(...); return n ? rt.codeHandlers(n) : null` — a non-existent path resolves to null and short-circuits to null (distinct from [] for a real node with no handlers).
specs.expect: Returns null (NOT []), distinguishing 'node not found' from 'node found, zero code handlers'.

specs.title: snapshot({relevant:true}) attaches codeHandlers and keeps code-only nodes
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene.
specs.groundTruth: core.snapshot keeps a node under `relevant` when btn||label||hasCode, and emits desc.codeHandlers when codeHandlers is non-empty. Cross-checked against listeners() for the same refs (same authored script source).
specs.expect: The ClickCodeBtn descriptor carries codeHandlers:[{type:'click',fn:'onClick',target:'CopseClickHandler'}]; the non-button Overlay node is PRESENT (kept by relevant via hasCode) with its codeHandlers and no `button`; each node's codeHandlers equals its listeners() output.

specs.title: press a code-on('click') button => drove:['click'], handler runs, NO synthetic touch
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. ClickCodeBtn has empty clickEvents + on('click')→onClick(count++); count starts 0 (authored via cocos-creator @property).
specs.groundTruth: The fixture script's deterministic logic: onClick increments count by exactly 1 per click. Initial 0 was authored by cocos-creator MCP (@property). press fires no serialized clickEvent (fired:0) but emitClick reaches the on('click'); droveClick=true so emitTouch is skipped.
specs.expect: press returns { ok:true, fired:0, drove:['click'] } with NO 'touch' in drove and no `touched` field; count reads 0 before and 1 after (handler actually ran).

specs.title: press a touch-wired button => drove:['touch'], wired:true, synthetic tap reaches the TOUCH_END handler
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. TouchCodeBtn has empty clickEvents + on(TOUCH_END)→onTouch(count++); count starts 0.
specs.groundTruth: Fixture deterministic logic: onTouch increments count by 1 on a TOUCH_END at the node. press: no clickEvent (fired:0), no on('click') (droveClick=false) → emitTouch synthesizes touch-start→touch-end at node centre; wired=true because a code handler exists.
specs.expect: press returns { ok:true, fired:0, touched:true, drove:['touch'], wired:true }; count reads 0 before and 1 after — proving the synthetic tap actuated the raw TOUCH_END handler (the headline emitTouch capability).

specs.title: press a node wired on('click')+on(TOUCH_START) => drove:['click'] only; touch handler NOT fired
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. MultiCodeBtn: on('click')→clickCount++, on(TOUCH_START)→touchCount++; both start 0.
specs.action: get clickCount & touchCount (both 0) → mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/MultiCodeBtn'} → get Canvas/MultiCodeBtn:CopseMultiHandler.clickCount and .touchCount
specs.groundTruth: core.press only synthesizes a tap when `fired===0 && !droveClick`. Here droveClick=true (an on('click') exists), so emitTouch is NOT called → the touch-start handler must not fire. Oracle = fixture's two independent counters.
specs.expect: press drove === ['click'] (no 'touch'); clickCount 0→1 but touchCount stays 0 — the droveClick gate correctly suppressed the synthetic touch.

specs.title: press honours interactable for a code-on('click') button; force:true overrides
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. GuardedBtn: interactable=false (authored via cocos-creator), on('click')→onClick(count++), count starts 0.
specs.action: mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/GuardedBtn'} → get count (expect still 0) → mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/GuardedBtn', force:true} → get Canvas/GuardedBtn:CopseClickHandler.count (expect 1)
specs.groundTruth: interactable=false was authored by cocos-creator MCP. core.press returns {ok:false, reason:'disabled'} unless force, and the fixture's onClick increments count by 1 only when actually invoked.
specs.expect: First press: { ok:false, reason:'disabled' } and count stays 0 (handler NOT run). Forced press: { ok:true, drove:['click'] } and count becomes 1.

specs.title: press a button wired to NOTHING => wired:false, touch-into-void (no tree change)
specs.surface: core.press (wired computation; emitTouch on live engine); mcp__copse__press
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. DeadBtn is a bare Button: empty clickEvents, no script, no code handlers.
specs.groundTruth: Fixture: DeadBtn has zero handlers (clickHandlers empty + codeHandlers empty) → core.press computes wired=false. On the live preview engine emitTouch successfully dispatches (EventTouch/Touch/dispatchEvent exist) so drove becomes ['touch'] (touch-into-void); drove:'nothing' only occurs when emitTouch is unavailable. The `changed` settle-diff must be empty (nothing actuated).
specs.expect: press returns wired:false AND ( drove === 'nothing'  OR  (Array.isArray(drove) && drove includes 'touch') ); `changed` (appeared/activated/labelChanged/…) is all-empty — nothing in the tree moved.

specs.title: press => drove:'nothing', wired:false when no handler and emitTouch unavailable (core contract)
specs.setup: Library/core test over the fake tree from test/core.test.js — a Button with clickEvents:[] and a Runtime WITHOUT emitTouch (so the touch fallback can't fire). Cannot be reliably produced on the live preview, where runtime.emitTouch always exists.
specs.action: press(scene, rt, 'Canvas/TouchBtn')  // rt has no emitTouch; node has empty clickEvents and no code handlers
specs.groundTruth: core.press: fired===0 && !droveClick && typeof rt.emitTouch!=='function' → drove stays [] → res.drove='nothing'; with no clickHandlers/codeHandlers → wired=false. This is a contract assertion (the dead-button signal the harness driveGate keys on), no external oracle.
specs.expect: press returns { ok:true, ref:'Canvas/TouchBtn', fired:0, drove:'nothing', wired:false } (matches the existing test/core.test.js shape minus the touch fallback).

specs.title: hijack() patches Node.prototype.on once and is idempotent
specs.setup: Connect to a FRESH CodeScene session (no prior hijack this session).
specs.groundTruth: hijack() guards on N.on.__copseHijacked: first call wraps on/off and returns already:false; subsequent calls detect the marker and return already:true without re-wrapping (contract in the source).
specs.expect: First call returns { ok:true, already:false }; second returns { ok:true, already:true }.

specs.title: captured() shows a node.on() registration made AFTER hijack
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene. LazyNode has CopseLazyWire which does NOT wire in start(); wireNow() runs this.node.on('click', this.onLate, this).
specs.groundTruth: Script source CopseLazyWire.wireNow wires exactly one on('click')→onLate. hijack records registrations made after it runs into the _captured WeakMap; before wireNow there is nothing to capture.
specs.expect: captured() is [] before; after hijack+wireNow captured() returns [{ type:'click', fn:'onLate', target:'CopseLazyWire' }] and listeners() (live processor) also now includes that entry.

specs.title: captured() does NOT show registrations made before hijack (temporal boundary)
specs.surface: src/cocos/runtime.js hijack (post-install only); mcp__copse__captured vs listeners
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene, then mcp__copse__hijack {} (AFTER scene load — ClickCodeBtn already wired its on('click') in start() at load time, before hijack).
specs.groundTruth: The documented hijack caveat: it only captures registrations made AFTER it patches on(). ClickCodeBtn wired at scene load (before hijack) → absent from captured but present in listeners (which reads the live NodeEventProcessor regardless of when it was wired).
specs.expect: captured('Canvas/ClickCodeBtn') === [] (load-time wiring not recorded) WHILE listeners('Canvas/ClickCodeBtn') still returns the on('click') — proving the 'only after hijack' boundary and the listeners↔captured division of labour.

specs.title: captured() on an unresolvable ref returns null
specs.setup: Connect to CodeScene; hijack optional.
specs.groundTruth: install.captured does `const n = resolve(...); return n ? readCaptured(n) : null` — a miss returns null, distinct from [] for a resolved node with no captured registrations.
specs.expect: Returns null (not []).

specs.title: runHarness hard-fails a press that drove NOTHING (driveGate)
specs.setup: Library test: localDriver/fake driver whose press returns { ok:true, fired:0, drove:'nothing', wired:false }; deterministic agent that presses once and a judge/report that both try to PASS.
specs.action: runHarness(driver, agent)  then  runHarness(driver, agent, { driveGate:false })
specs.groundTruth: harness.js: a step with result.drove==='nothing' is collected into undrivenPresses and forces out.pass=false (overriding judge+report) unless driveGate:false. Deterministic fake — contract assertion (this is the dead-button gate, matching test/harness.test.js).
specs.expect: First run: report.pass===false and report.undriven===[{ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}] even though judge+report returned pass:true. Second run (driveGate:false): report.pass===true.

specs.title: runHarness SURFACES a synthetic-tap-into-void (drove:['touch'], wired:false) as uncertain, not a hard fail
specs.setup: Library test: fake driver whose press returns { ok:true, fired:0, touched:true, drove:['touch'], wired:false }; agent presses once with a passing judge.
specs.groundTruth: harness.js execStep tags result.uncertain='touch-into-void' when drove is exactly ['touch'] and wired===false (copse may simply not see the touch handler), and runHarness collects it into out.uncertain WITHOUT failing — fail-loud-but-not-fatal contract (matches test/harness.test.js).
specs.expect: report.pass===true (NOT hard-failed) and report.uncertain===[{ ref:'Canvas/...', why:'touch-into-void' }]; the step result carries uncertain:'touch-into-void'.

specs.title: MCP `listeners` tool returns the same code-handler descriptors as the library
specs.setup: Connected CodeScene session.
specs.action: mcp__copse__listeners { ref:'Canvas/MultiCodeBtn' } compared against a library snapshot of the same node's codeHandlers (via mcp__copse__snapshot relevant:true).
specs.groundTruth: The MCP listeners tool is a 1:1 pass-through to the in-page __copse.listeners (= rt.codeHandlers). Its output must equal the codeHandlers field the snapshot tool attaches for the same ref (both read the same NodeEventProcessor).
specs.expect: mcp listeners result deep-equals the codeHandlers array on the MultiCodeBtn descriptor from mcp snapshot (same {type,fn,target} set).

specs.title: MCP hijack + captured tools round-trip a post-hijack registration
specs.setup: Fresh connected CodeScene session.
specs.groundTruth: Same temporal contract as code-captured-after-hijack but exercised entirely through the MCP tool surface; oracle = the wireNow() script source.
specs.expect: captured tool returns [{ type:'click', fn:'onLate', target:'CopseLazyWire' }]; an identical sequence WITHOUT the hijack call first returns [].

specs.title: CLI single-shot `copse press` reports drove for a code-on('click') button
specs.setup: Preview serving CodeScene at http://localhost:7456.
specs.groundTruth: The CLI press path calls cp.press(ref) and prints the raw core.press JSON. For ClickCodeBtn (on('click'), no clickEvents) core.press yields drove:['click'] — same logic as code-press-drove-click, just over the shell entrypoint.
specs.expect: stdout is JSON with { ok:true, ref:'Canvas/ClickCodeBtn', fired:0, drove:['click'] } (exit 0); no 'touch' in drove.

specs.title: Code-registered on('click') is visible to copse but INVISIBLE to coir's static map (complementarity)
specs.surface: core.listeners / clickSurface vs coir static ClickEvent map
specs.setup: Same CodeScene project loaded in both copse (runtime, preview) and coir (static, mcp__coir__*).
specs.action: mcp__copse__listeners {ref:'Canvas/ClickCodeBtn'}  AND  mcp__copse__click_surface {}  vs  mcp__coir__get / mcp__coir__find for the ClickCodeBtn node's clickEvents.
specs.groundTruth: coir reads only serialized editor-wired clickEvents; ClickCodeBtn has EMPTY clickEvents and wires its handler in code (node.on('click')). So coir reports zero ClickEvent edges for it, while copse listeners reports the live handler and clickSurface emits method:null with a codeHandlers ride-along — demonstrating copse covers a surface coir structurally cannot see.
specs.expect: coir shows ClickCodeBtn with no clickEvent/handler edge; copse listeners returns the on('click'); copse click_surface row for Canvas/ClickCodeBtn has method:null AND carries codeHandlers (so coverageJoin would bucket it codeRegistered, not covered).

specs.title: fn/target names are present on the dev preview; release-build minification strips them (documented limit)
specs.setup: Connect to the CodeScene PREVIEW (non-minified dev build).
specs.action: mcp__copse__listeners { ref:'Canvas/ClickCodeBtn' } — inspect the fn/target fields.
specs.groundTruth: codeHandlers derives fn from ci.callback.name and target from ci.target.constructor.name. On a dev/preview build these are the real names ('onClick','CopseClickHandler'); on a minified release build they mangle to single letters or undefined — a KNOWN limit (you keep listener identity, not semantics). This spec pins the dev-build behaviour and records the release caveat; it is NOT runnable against a minified build via this rig.
specs.expect: On the preview: fn==='onClick' and target==='CopseClickHandler' (defined, human-readable). Documented expectation (untested here): a release build would return fn/target as undefined or mangled while `type` and listener identity/count remain correct.

specs.title: Nested node path resolves to the right node (member readback)
specs.setup: copse connected to preview serving SelScene; Canvas/ScoreLabel has Label.string='hi' authored via cocos-creator MCP.
specs.groundTruth: Value authored via cocos-creator (component_set_component_property Label.string='hi'); cross-confirm with cocos-creator node_get_node_info / coir get on ScoreLabel.
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:'hi' } — resolve walked Canvas/ScoreLabel and read the authored Label.string.

specs.title: Top-level child resolves (root itself is not a path segment)
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene preview.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator scene_get_scene_hierarchy: 'Canvas' is a direct child of the scene root.
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:'Canvas' } — a single top-level segment resolves (paths are scene-root-relative).

specs.title: Unknown path segment -> resolve null -> not-found
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene; no node named 'Nope'.
specs.groundTruth: coir find 'Nope' on the same scene returns nothing; cocos-creator node_find_node_by_name 'Nope' empty.
specs.expect: { ok:false, ref:'Canvas/Nope:Label.string', reason:'not-found' } — no throw; resolve returned null and get short-circuited.

specs.title: Bare same-name path = first sibling ([0]); silent first-pick (coir refuses)
specs.setup: Three 'Item' siblings authored with Label.string item0/item1/item2 in child order.
specs.groundTruth: Authored item0 on the first Item (cocos-creator child order). docs/SELECTORS.md + selectors.test.js pin copse's silent [0] pick (divergence: coir refuses an ambiguous bare name).
specs.expect: Both return { ok:true, value:'item0' } — bare 'Item' and 'Item[0]' resolve to the SAME (first) sibling; copse does not refuse the ambiguity.

specs.title: [i] is 0-based among same-name siblings and resolves DISTINCT nodes
specs.setup: Three 'Item' siblings, Label.string item0/item1/item2.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator authored each Item's Label.string by child index; the distinct strings prove [0]/[1]/[2] hit distinct authored nodes (the canonical 'two same-name siblings -> [0]/[1] distinct' oracle).
specs.expect: Values 'item0','item1','item2' respectively — index maps to child-order position among same-name siblings.

specs.title: Out-of-range [i] -> null -> not-found
specs.setup: Exactly three 'Item' siblings exist.
specs.groundTruth: coir/cocos-creator confirm exactly 3 'Item' siblings under Canvas (count=3, so index 3 is out of range).
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } — same[3] is undefined, resolve returns null.

specs.title: Trailing [i] is ALWAYS an index — copse cannot address a node literally named 'Cell[0]'
specs.setup: A node literally named 'Cell[0]' exists; NO sibling named 'Cell'.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator node_find_node_by_name 'Cell[0]' (and coir find) confirm the node EXISTS — yet copse's resolve parses the trailing [0] as an index, looks for 'Cell'[0] (no 'Cell' sibling), and fails. Documented divergence (docs/SELECTORS.md, selectors.test.js).
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } despite the node existing — pins the no-literal-bracket divergence (coir would match it via full-path-first).

specs.title: #N absolute index unsupported (treated as a node NAME)
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene; no node named '#0'.
specs.groundTruth: docs/SELECTORS.md + selectors.test.js: '#N' is parsed as a literal name (the bracket regex doesn't fire); nothing is named '#0'. No stable runtime index exists.
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } — '#0' is not an index, just an unmatched name.

specs.title: Paths are scene-root-relative — prefixing the scene name fails
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene (scene root's direct child is Canvas).
specs.groundTruth: coir emits paths carrying the scene/prefab-file root (e.g. 'SelScene/Canvas/...'); copse roots at the scene-root's children and has no child named 'SelScene' — exactly the rooting divergence resolveCoirPath/dropped absorbs (docs/COVERAGE.md, docs/SELECTORS.md).
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } — the scene-name prefix is not a segment; contrast SEL-interop-coir-path which translates it.

specs.title: Empty path resolves to the root, which resolve maps to null
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene.
specs.groundTruth: Contract in src/core/index.js resolve(): split('').filter(Boolean) is empty, the loop never runs, cur stays === root, and `cur === root ? null` returns null. The scene root itself is intentionally not addressable.
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } — the root node has no ref.

specs.title: Leading / trailing / doubled slashes are ignored (filter(Boolean))
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene.
specs.action: mcp__copse__node { ref: "/Canvas//ScoreLabel" } compared against mcp__copse__node { ref: "Canvas/ScoreLabel" }
specs.groundTruth: Contract: resolve splits on '/' then filter(Boolean) drops empty segments, so the two refs are equivalent. Both must report identical node intrinsics for the same authored node.
specs.expect: Both succeed (ok:true) and return identical node info — empty path segments are normalized away.

specs.title: Non-numeric bracket is NOT an index — treated as part of the literal name
specs.setup: Three 'Item' siblings; no node named 'Item[x]'.
specs.groundTruth: Contract: resolve regex /^(.*?)(?:\[(\d+)\])?$/ — '[x]' fails the \d+ group, so name='Item[x]', idx=null, and no node is named 'Item[x]'. (Edge of the bracket parse.)
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'not-found' } — only [digits] is an index.

specs.title: Node pseudo-component reads an authored node intrinsic (active=false)
specs.setup: Canvas/Panel authored with node.active=false via cocos-creator (node_set_node_property active=false).
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator node_get_node_info on Panel reports active=false (the authored flag).
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:false } — get's comp==='Node' branch read node.active, not a component member.

specs.title: Node pseudo-component on an active node (active=true)
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel active (default).
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator node_get_node_info on ScoreLabel reports active=true.
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:true } — Node pseudo reads the authored active flag.

specs.title: resolve is structure-based, not active-based — walks into an inactive subtree
specs.setup: Canvas/Panel.active=false; its child PanelInner.active=true with Label.string='inner'.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator authored PanelInner.Label.string='inner' under an inactive Panel. resolve uses rt.children (not isActive), so it descends regardless of active (unlike snapshot which prunes inactive).
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:'inner' } — resolve reaches a node under an inactive parent.

specs.title: get on a node lacking the requested component -> no-component
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel has a Label but no Button.
specs.groundTruth: coir get / cocos-creator component_get_components on ScoreLabel list no cc.Button.
specs.expect: { ok:false, reason:'no-component' } — resolve hit the node, getComponent('Button') returned null.

specs.title: Malformed member selector (no ':') throws
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene.
specs.groundTruth: Contract: splitMember throws Error /:Comp\.member/ when there is no ':'. selectors.test.js pins this. MCP server.js surfaces a thrown handler error as an isError tool result.
specs.expect: MCP tool result is an error (isError) whose message matches ':Comp.member' — not an {ok:false} value.

specs.title: Malformed member selector (no '.' after ':') throws
specs.setup: copse connected to SelScene.
specs.groundTruth: Contract: splitMember throws Error /Comp\.member/ when there is no '.' in the part after ':'. selectors.test.js pins this.
specs.expect: MCP tool result is an error whose message matches 'Comp.member'.

specs.title: Component name tolerant of the cc. prefix (cc.Label === Label)
specs.surface: core get + runtime getComponent via mcp__copse__get
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel has cc.Label string='hi'.
specs.groundTruth: Authored Label.string='hi'. runtime getComponent tries the name as-given, then cc[stripCc(type)], then the stripped name — so 'cc.Label' and 'Label' both resolve to the same component (cross-version tolerance).
specs.expect: Both return { ok:true, value:'hi' } — the cc. prefix is stripped/fallback-resolved.

specs.title: Member after the FIRST dot is taken verbatim (no nested navigation)
specs.setup: Canvas/ScoreLabel has a Label.
specs.groundTruth: Contract: splitMember uses indexOf('.') (first dot) -> comp='Label', member='node.name'; readProp does c['node.name'] (flat key access), which does NOT navigate label.node.name. Pins the documented 'drill into the returned value yourself' limitation.
specs.expect: { ok:true, value:undefined } (or null) — copse does NOT traverse nested members; it read the flat key 'node.name'.

specs.title: call drives path:Comp.method(...args) with a deterministic delta
specs.setup: Canvas/Mgr has CopseCounter (count:number, add(n){count+=n;return count}).
specs.action: mcp__copse__get { sel: "Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count" } (c0) ; mcp__copse__call { sel: "Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.add", args:[5] } ; mcp__copse__get { sel: "Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count" }
specs.groundTruth: Fixture script deterministic logic: add(n) returns the new count = prior + n and stores it. Predicted delta = c0 + 5, independent of copse's own readback.
specs.expect: call returns { ok:true, value: c0+5 } and the follow-up get reads c0+5 — call resolved the node, found CopseCounter, and invoked add with the arg.

Pins that a coir path that would NOT resolve raw (SEL-scene-root-relative) becomes the exact runtime ref after translation.
specs.expect: resolve returns { ref:'Canvas/ScoreLabel', mount:'', dropped:'SelScene' } (single hit, not ambiguous/null) and the subsequent get returns { ok:true, value:'hi' }.

specs.title: Shared-core [i] node paths from coir resolve in copse to distinct nodes

specs.groundTruth: Interop corpus contract (selectors.test.js COIR_EMITS): explicit [i] paths transfer for same-name siblings. Oracle = authored item0/item2 (cocos-creator child order). Note the docs caveat that [i] can drift; here unique authored strings confirm the mapping held.
specs.expect: get returns 'item0' and 'item2' — coir-shaped [i] paths resolve to the matching authored siblings in copse.

specs.title: Real script class name resolves on the (non-minified) preview; minification is a release-build-only divergence

Documents the boundary honestly rather than asserting a minified name we can't produce here.

specs.title: a disabled press does NOT fire its handler (count unchanged vs a known-mutating control)
specs.setup: connect + reload (count→authored 0). Sequence is stateful; run in order against one session.
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__get {sel:'Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count'} → baseline. 2) CONTROL: mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/AddBtn'}; mcp__copse__get count. 3) GUARD: mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/DisabledBtn'}; mcp__copse__get count.
specs.groundTruth: Deterministic fixture logic: CopseClickBump.onLoad wires on(CLICK)→CopseCounter.add(1); add increments by exactly 1. The control press (step 2) demonstrates the instrument moves the needle (0→1); a disabled press returns before emitClick (line 188), so step 3 MUST leave count at the control value. Oracle = the script's known semantics, NOT copse re-reading itself (the control proves copse get detects real mutations).
specs.expect: Step 2: press ok:true, count 0→1. Step 3: press {ok:false,reason:'disabled'} AND count still 1 (NOT 2) — the disabled handler never ran.

specs.title: force overrides disabled and DOES fire (proves the disabled handler is real)
specs.setup: connect + reload (count→0). Run after establishing baseline.
specs.action: mcp__copse__get {sel:'Canvas/Mgr:CopseCounter.count'} (baseline N); mcp__copse__press {ref:'Canvas/DisabledBtn', force:true}; mcp__copse__get count.
specs.groundTruth: With force, line 188's guard is skipped → fireClickHandlers + emitClick run → CopseClickBump bumps add(1). Deterministic fixture logic: count rises by exactly 1. This is the complement that makes guard-press-disabled-noop meaningful (the handler IS wired; only the guard suppressed it).
specs.expect: press → {ok:true, ref:'Canvas/DisabledBtn', drove:['click'] (fired:0; no serialized clickEvents)} and count === N+1.

specs.action: BEFORE: mcp__cocos-creator__scene_get_scene_hierarchy + component_get_component_info on Canvas/Mgr(CopseCounter.count), Canvas/CountLabel(Label.string), Canvas/AddBtn & Canvas/DisabledBtn(Button.interactable). Run all guard specs. AFTER: repeat the same readbacks.

This is the lens's 'fixture untouched' backbone (cross-tool).
specs.expect: AFTER readback === BEFORE readback for every captured field (count 0, label '0', AddBtn.interactable true, DisabledBtn.interactable false, identical node tree) — no guard spec perturbed the authored fixture.

specs.title: Join key (path-tail, method) agrees byte-for-byte across copse runtime and coir static on the same scene
specs.surface: clickSurface (copse) x coir ClickEvent map; coverageJoin
specs.setup: Open assets/_copsetest/CopseTest.scene in the editor so preview:7456 serves it. mcp__copse__connect {url:'http://localhost:7456'}. The scene's editor-wired buttons (AddBtn->addScore, CoveredBtn->claim, OccludedBtn->claim2, Panel/CloseBtn->closePanel) are the corpus.
specs.action: 1) mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} -> save rows to scratchpad/run.json. 2) For each button, mcp__coir__find {name:'<btn>'} then mcp__coir__deps/analyze on it to read its `click -> <method>()` edge -> assemble [{nodePath,method,handlerClass}] to scratchpad/coir.json. 3) node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs and compare: build the set of (tail-of-ref, method) from copse rows with method!=null against the set of (tail-of-nodePath, method) from coir, where tail strips the differing scene-root/mount head (use resolveCoirPath to align each coir path to its copse ref).
specs.groundTruth: coir reading the SAME .scene file from disk (independent tool, static): the serialized handler method string is identical in both tools because it survives as a plain string in the scene JSON. cocos-creator MCP authored each handler name. Three-way independent agreement.
specs.expect: Every copse row with method!=null whose ref aligns (via resolveCoirPath) to a coir nodePath has an EQUAL method string to coir's ClickEvent for that node; the aligned (path,method) sets are identical. No copse-wired method missing from coir and vice-versa (modulo touch/code-only rows, which are method:null).

specs.title: AddBtn -> COVERED via symmetric tail (coir path carries the scene-root head copse drops)
specs.setup: Same live rig as xtool-join-key-integrity; AddBtn is reachable (no overlay), interactable, active.
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} -> find row {ref:'Canvas/AddBtn', method:'addScore', reachable:true, interactable:true}. coir: mcp__coir__find {name:'AddBtn'} -> nodePath (expected 'CopseTest/Canvas/AddBtn'); mcp__coir__deps on it -> method 'addScore', handlerClass 'CopseCtrl'. Then node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs runs coverageJoin([{nodePath:<coir AddBtn>,method:'addScore',handlerClass:'CopseCtrl'}], <copse rows>).

specs.expect: result.covered contains exactly one row with method 'addScore'; its via==='prefix', mount==='', dropped===coir's root segment (the scene-file name copse omits, e.g. 'CopseTest'); runtime.ref==='Canvas/AddBtn'; handlerClass 'CopseCtrl' carried through from coir. AddBtn appears in NO other bucket.

specs.title: CoveredBtn -> BLOCKED: coir says wired, copse reachable:false names the input-consuming overlay
specs.setup: Live rig. Canvas/Overlay (cc.Sprite + cc.BlockInputEvents) is a sibling drawn AFTER CoveredBtn, covering its center. CoveredBtn is interactable & active (wiring is intact; only reachability fails).
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__reachable {sel:'Canvas/CoveredBtn'} -> expect {reachable:false, blockedBy:'Canvas/Overlay'}; mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} -> row for CoveredBtn carries reachable:false. coir: find+deps CoveredBtn -> {nodePath, method:'claim'}. node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs runs coverageJoin([{nodePath:<coir CoveredBtn>,method:'claim'}], <copse rows>).
specs.groundTruth: coir confirms CoveredBtn IS statically wired (so it is NOT 'unreached'); cocos-creator authored the Overlay's z-order ON TOP with BlockInputEvents (an input consumer). The combined verdict 'wired+live-but-not-reachable=blocked' is what the join must produce. (The reachable heuristic itself is copse-internal; the wiring and z-order are independently authored/coir-confirmed.)
specs.expect: result.blocked contains the CoveredBtn row with method 'claim' and runtime.blockedBy==='Canvas/Overlay'; CoveredBtn is NOT in covered, unreached, uncertain, codeOnly, or ambiguous.

specs.title: OccludedBtn -> UNCERTAIN: input reaches (reachable:true) but an opaque sprite occludes it -> NOT a confident covered
specs.setup: Live rig. Canvas/OpaqueImg (opaque cc.Sprite, NO BlockInputEvents) drawn after OccludedBtn over its center. OccludedBtn wired -> claim2, interactable, active.
specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__reachable {sel:'Canvas/OccludedBtn'} -> expect {reachable:true, occludedBy:'Canvas/OpaqueImg'}; mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} -> OccludedBtn row carries reachable:true + occludedBy. coir: find+deps -> {nodePath, method:'claim2'}. node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs runs coverageJoin([{nodePath:<coir OccludedBtn>,method:'claim2'}], <copse rows>).
specs.groundTruth: coir confirms wiring; cocos-creator authored an OPAQUE renderer with no input consumer on top (z-order). copse's documented semantics: input ignores opacity so reachable stays true, but occludedBy is set -> the join must keep it OUT of covered (fail-loud uncertainty).
specs.expect: result.uncertain contains the OccludedBtn row (occludedBy==='Canvas/OpaqueImg'); covered does NOT contain it; blocked does NOT contain it (reachable is true, not false).

specs.title: Panel/CloseBtn -> UNREACHED: coir-only (statically wired, not live because Panel is inactive)

specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} (default includeInactive:false) -> assert NO row with ref ending 'CloseBtn'. coir: mcp__coir__find {name:'CloseBtn'} -> nodePath present in the static scene tree; deps -> method 'closePanel'. node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs runs coverageJoin([{nodePath:<coir CloseBtn>,method:'closePanel'}], <copse rows>).
specs.groundTruth: coir's static scene tree includes CloseBtn (active state does not remove it from the scene file) while cocos-creator authored Panel.active=false; copse's runtime omits inactive subtrees by default. coir-present + copse-absent is the exact definition of unreached.
specs.expect: result.unreached contains the raw static row {nodePath:<coir CloseBtn>, method:'closePanel'}; CloseBtn is in NO other bucket; copse click_surface returned zero rows for CloseBtn.

specs.groundTruth: Deterministic, oracle = cocos-creator authored the active flip (independent of copse). coir static rows are unchanged; only copse's live set changed. The bucket transition unreached->covered is forced by the authored activation.
specs.expect: In the second join CloseBtn is in result.covered (via='prefix', method 'closePanel') and NO LONGER in result.unreached; the only thing that changed between the two joins is the authored Panel.active.

specs.title: TouchBtn (no clickEvent, no node.on) -> CODE-ONLY; coir confirms zero ClickEvent edges

specs.action: copse: mcp__copse__click_surface {reachability:true} -> row {ref:'Canvas/TouchBtn', method:null} with no codeHandlers. coir: mcp__coir__find {name:'TouchBtn'} exists, mcp__coir__deps shows NO `click -> method()` script edge. node scratchpad/_xtool-join.mjs runs coverageJoin(<coir rows WITHOUT a TouchBtn entry>, <copse rows>).

specs.groundTruth: coir is BLIND to code-registered handlers (they aren't in the scene JSON) — copse's listeners/codeHandlers is the only lens, and the join must downgrade not promote them. Oracle for 'this button has a code handler' = the authored CodeBtnReg script source (independent of copse's readback) + copse listeners agreeing with codeHandlers.
specs.expect: result.codeRegistered contains the CodeBtn row (codeHandlers non-empty); result.codeOnly contains TouchBtn; NEITHER is in covered (registration alone is not proof of a real action handler).

The mount ('Canvas/List') is something coir literally cannot know — it is the live instantiation parent that copse observed. The join inferring it from the live tree is the cross-tool value.
specs.expect: result.covered contains a row with method 'tap', via==='prefix', mount==='Canvas/List' (the segments above the matched suffix), dropped==='' (the static path was fully consumed), runtime.ref==='Canvas/List/CopseRow/Btn'.

specs.title: Same-name sibling [i] ordering: copse runtime vs coir static (agreement, or documented drift the join strips)

The exact tier is byte-equal (would MISS on drift); the prefix tier rescues it.

specs.surface: src/mcp/tools.js names vs src/drivers/puppeteer.js cp object
specs.setup: Pure import.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {TOOLS} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/tools.js'; const driver=new Set(['reload','snapshot','interactive','clickSurface','press','call','get','reachable','node','diff','listeners','hijack','captured','logs','close']); const special=new Set(['connect','resolve']); const camel=s=>s.replace(/_([a-z])/g,(_,c)=>c.toUpperCase()); const orphan=TOOLS.filter(t=>!t.debug).map(t=>t.name).filter(n=>!special.has(n)&&!driver.has(camel(n))); console.log(JSON.stringify({orphan}))"
specs.groundTruth: The cp Driver object literal in src/drivers/puppeteer.js (its method keys) is the library surface — the independent oracle for the docs' '1:1 with the library' claim. click_surface↔clickSurface is the only casing divergence.
specs.expect: orphan===[] — every non-debug tool name except connect/resolve maps (after snake→camel) to a real Driver method.

specs.title: Exactly the 7 CDP Debugger tools carry debug:true
specs.setup: Pure import.
specs.groundTruth: Source: the debug:true tag is set on break_at/break_in/break_exceptions/wait_pause/eval_frame/debug_step/clear_breakpoints and nothing else — the set server.js filters out of tools/list.
specs.expect: Sorted set === [break_at,break_exceptions,break_in,clear_breakpoints,debug_step,eval_frame,wait_pause] (7 entries, no testing primitive tagged).

specs.title: Required-param declarations match each tool's mandatory args
specs.setup: Pure import.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {TOOLS} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/tools.js'; const want={press:['ref'],get:['sel'],call:['sel'],resolve:['path'],reachable:['ref'],node:['ref'],captured:['ref'],listeners:['ref'],diff:['before','after'],break_at:['urlRegex','line'],break_in:['sel'],break_exceptions:['state'],eval_frame:['frame','expr'],debug_step:['kind']}; const m=new Map(TOOLS.map(t=>[t.name,t.inputSchema.required||[]])); const mism=Object.entries(want).filter(([n,r])=>JSON.stringify(m.get(n))!==JSON.stringify(r)); console.log(JSON.stringify({mism}))"
specs.groundTruth: Each tool's run() body dereferences exactly those args (e.g. press uses a.ref, diff uses a.before/a.after) — the source is the oracle. Tools with all-optional args (snapshot/interactive/reload/logs/hijack/connect/close) declare no `required`.
specs.expect: mism===[] — every listed tool's inputSchema.required equals its mandatory-arg list.

specs.title: initialize → serverInfo {name:'copse', version from package.json}, capabilities.tools present
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher, no session.
specs.groundTruth: package.json `version` field (0.0.1) is the independent oracle for serverInfo.version; the MCP spec requires serverInfo.name + a capabilities object. createDispatcher reads VERSION from package.json at module load.
specs.expect: result.serverInfo.name==='copse', result.serverInfo.version==='0.0.1' (===package.json version), result.capabilities.tools is an object, typeof result.protocolVersion==='string'.

specs.title: initialize echoes the client's protocolVersion, else defaults to 2025-06-18
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const h=createDispatcher({cp:null}); const a=await h({id:1,method:'initialize',params:{protocolVersion:'2024-11-05'}}); const b=await h({id:2,method:'initialize',params:{}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({echoed:a.result.protocolVersion, def:b.result.protocolVersion}))"
specs.groundTruth: server.js line: `const pv = (msg.params && msg.params.protocolVersion) || PROTOCOL_VERSION;` with PROTOCOL_VERSION='2025-06-18'. Source contract.
specs.expect: echoed==='2024-11-05' (client value honoured) and def==='2025-06-18' (default when omitted).

specs.title: ping → empty result object
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher.
specs.groundTruth: MCP/JSON-RPC ping contract: `case 'ping': return { result: {} }`. Source.
specs.expect: r deepEquals {result:{}}.

specs.title: notifications/initialized and notifications/cancelled produce no reply
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const h=createDispatcher({cp:null}); console.log(JSON.stringify({a:await h({method:'notifications/initialized'}), b:await h({method:'notifications/cancelled'})}))"
specs.groundTruth: JSON-RPC: notifications (no id) get no response; server.js returns null for these two methods and send() skips null. Source.
specs.expect: a===null and b===null (handle returns null; the server sends nothing).

specs.title: Unknown method: -32601 when it has an id; null (no reply) when it's a notification
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const h=createDispatcher({cp:null}); const req=await h({id:9,method:'frobnicate'}); const note=await h({method:'frobnicate'}); console.log(JSON.stringify({req:req.error, note}))"
specs.groundTruth: server.js default: `msg.id !== undefined ? { error: { code: -32601, ... } } : null`. JSON-RPC 'method not found' is -32601; id-less requests are notifications. Source.
specs.expect: req.code===-32601 and req.message matches /method not found: frobnicate/; note===null.

specs.title: tools/list hides the 7 debug tools by default (17 advertised)

specs.groundTruth: server.js: `const advertised = state.debug ? TOOLS : TOOLS.filter(t=>!t.debug)`. Source. Pins test/mcp.test.js 'tools/list hides debug tools' case.

specs.title: tools/call wraps a Driver {data} result as MCP text content, JSON intact (changed survives)
specs.setup: createDispatcher over a fake cp whose press returns {ok,fired,changed}.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const cp={calls:[],press(ref,o){this.calls.push(['press',ref,o]);return{ok:true,ref,fired:1,changed:{appeared:[{ref:'Canvas/Panel'}]}}}}; const r=await createDispatcher({cp})({id:4,method:'tools/call',params:{name:'press',arguments:{ref:'Canvas/Btn'}}}); const p=JSON.parse(r.result.content[0].text); console.log(JSON.stringify({call:cp.calls.at(-1), ok:p.ok, appeared:p.changed.appeared[0].ref, isError:r.result.isError}))"
specs.groundTruth: server.js: success path is `return toolResult(JSON.stringify(res.data))`; content[0] is {type:'text', text}. Pins test/mcp.test.js 'tools/call dispatches' (auto-delta survives the round-trip).
specs.expect: call===['press','Canvas/Btn',{}], ok===true, appeared==='Canvas/Panel', isError===undefined.

specs.title: call tool spreads args[] into the Driver call(sel, ...args)

specs.title: tools/call with an unknown tool name → JSON-RPC -32602 error (NOT an isError tool result)
specs.setup: createDispatcher with any cp.
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const r=await createDispatcher({cp:{}})({id:8,method:'tools/call',params:{name:'nope',arguments:{}}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({error:r.error, hasResult:'result' in r}))"
specs.groundTruth: server.js: `if (!tool) return { error: { code: -32602, message: 'unknown tool: ...' } }` — a protocol error, distinct from a tool-level isError. Source. Pins the test's unknown-tool case.
specs.expect: error.code===-32602, error.message matches /unknown tool: nope/, hasResult===false (it's a JSON-RPC error envelope, not a tool result).

specs.title: tools/call with params omitted → name undefined → -32602 'unknown tool: undefined'
specs.setup: Pure dispatcher.
specs.groundTruth: server.js: `const { name, arguments: args = {} } = msg.params || {}` → name undefined → TOOLS_BY_NAME.get(undefined) undefined → -32602. Edge branch in source.
specs.expect: error.code===-32602 and message matches /unknown tool: undefined/ (no crash on missing params).

specs.title: Calling a needCp tool with no open session → isError result, text /no open game/
specs.setup: createDispatcher({cp:null}).
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const h=createDispatcher({cp:null}); const out={}; for (const n of ['snapshot','press','get','reachable','reload','logs']) { const r=await h({id:1,method:'tools/call',params:{name:n,arguments:{ref:'X',sel:'X:Y.z',since:0}}}); out[n]={isError:r.result.isError, text:r.result.content[0].text}; } console.log(JSON.stringify(out))"
specs.groundTruth: tools.js needCp throws `no open game — call the connect tool with a url first` when state.cp is null; server.js catches → toolResult(text, true). This is the lens's central 'engine error → isError' contract. Pins the test's no-session case.
specs.expect: For every needCp tool: isError===true and text matches /no open game/ (prefixed with '✗ '). Live-equivalent VERIFIED THIS SESSION via the real process: '✗ no open game — call the `connect` tool with a url first'.

specs.title: A tool whose Driver throws → isError, text '✗ <msg>', and the ✗ is never doubled
specs.setup: Fake cp whose snapshot throws (two cases: plain message, and a message already starting with ✗).
specs.action: node --input-type=module -e "import {createDispatcher} from '/path/to/copse/src/mcp/server.js'; const mk=m=>({snapshot(){throw new Error(m)}}); const a=await createDispatcher({cp:mk('boom')})({id:1,method:'tools/call',params:{name:'snapshot',arguments:{}}}); const b=await createDispatcher({cp:mk('✗ already marked')})({id:2,method:'tools/call',params:{name:'snapshot',arguments:{}}}); console.log(JSON.stringify({a:{isError:a.result.isError,text:a.result.content[0].text}, b:b.result.content[0].text}))"
specs.groundTruth: server.js: `catch (e) { res = { error: e.message } }` then `toolResult('✗ '+error.replace(/^\s*✗\s*/,''), true)`. The regex strips a leading ✗ so it isn't doubled. Source.
specs.expect: a.isError===true and a.text==='✗ boom'; b==='✗ already marked' (single ✗, leading one stripped before re-prefixing).

specs.title: A successful {data} result carries no isError field
specs.setup: Fake cp with a get returning {ok,value}.
specs.groundTruth: server.js: success path omits isError entirely (`...(isError ? {isError:true} : {})`). Note: a Driver {ok:false} still goes through the {data} path (it's data, not a thrown error) — only thrown/{error} results set isError. Source.
specs.expect: isError===undefined (absent) and value==='42'.

specs.title: connect tool maps headed/fps/browserURL/attach+match → driver opts; target = url||match||''
specs.setup: Source-level review of the connect run body (the dynamic puppeteer import means it can't be exercised without launching Chrome).
specs.action: Read src/mcp/tools.js lines 48-65 and confirm: state.cp truthy → close+null (re-target); opts={...state.connectOpts}; a.headed→opts.headless=false; typeof a.fps==='number'→opts.fpsCap; a.browserURL→opts.browserURL; a.attach→opts.attach=true & opts.match=a.match||a.url; target=a.url||opts.match||''.
specs.groundTruth: The connect run() body in tools.js is the contract; downstream the mapped opts feed src/drivers/puppeteer.js connect(target, opts). connectOpts seeded from cli.js flags (--headed/--fps/--browser-url/--attach/--match) is spread first, so CLI flags become connect-tool defaults.
specs.expect: Each mapping holds as written; in particular attach with no url still derives target from match (target=opts.match) and `attached:!!opts.attach` reports the mode in the summary.

specs.title: connect (live, launch) returns a readiness summary whose button count matches the scene
specs.setup: Open assets/_copsetest/McpProbe.scene in Cocos Creator, start the preview (:7456). AddBtn is the only active button.
specs.groundTruth: Cocos Creator editor readback: mcp__cocos-creator__scene_get_scene_hierarchy (or coir mcp__coir__find Button) shows exactly 1 active cc.Button (AddBtn; Panel/CloseBtn is inactive). Independent of copse's own count.
specs.expect: Result {ok:true, url:'http://localhost:7456/', attached:false, relevantNodes>=2 (ScoreLabel+AddBtn), buttons===1} — buttons agrees with the editor/coir button count.

specs.title: connect on an already-open session closes the previous one (re-target, single live session)
specs.setup: Live preview at :7456 (McpProbe scene).
specs.action: mcp__copse__connect({url:"http://localhost:7456/"}) then mcp__copse__connect({url:"http://localhost:7456/"}) a second time, then mcp__copse__snapshot({})
specs.groundTruth: tools.js connect: `if (state.cp) { await state.cp.close(); state.cp = null; }` before opening the new one — docs/MCP.md 'One live session at a time; connect re-targets'. The second connect returning ok + a working snapshot proves the session was rebound, not duplicated/leaked.
specs.expect: Second connect → {ok:true, ...}; the subsequent snapshot succeeds (drives the NEW session). No error, no orphaned first session.

specs.title: connect to a non-Cocos / unreachable URL → tool.run throws → isError result
specs.setup: A URL with no Cocos engine (e.g. about:blank served page, or an unreachable port).
specs.groundTruth: connect() throws when no cc frame is found (bootInPage finds no engine → waitForFunction(__copse) times out) or navigation fails; server.js wraps the throw as an isError tool result. The 'engine limit, not the gate' note in docs/MCP.md. Independent of copse's read (it's the failure path).
specs.expect: isError===true with a text describing the inject/engine/navigation failure (e.g. /waiting|cc|navigation|timeout/), NOT a silent {ok:true}.

specs.title: connect attach to a renderer paused at a breakpoint → {paused:true, note} (inject deferred)
specs.setup: Your OWN dev build launched in a Chrome with --remote-debugging-port; pause the renderer in the debugger; then attach. (Low-frequency edge — needs the debugger; flagged as best-effort.)
specs.action: mcp__copse__connect({attach:true, browserURL:"http://127.0.0.1:9222", match:"<your-game>"})  while the renderer is halted
specs.groundTruth: tools.js: `if (state.cp.paused) return {data:{ok:true, attached:true, paused:true, note:...}}` — driver sets cp.paused when the inject was deferred because frame.evaluate blocked on a paused renderer (puppeteer.js pause auto-detect). Source branch.
specs.expect: Result {ok:true, attached:true, paused:true, note: contains 'renderer paused'} and NO relevantNodes/buttons (snapshot was deferred). Resuming then makes snapshot/press work.

specs.title: A debug tool with no open session → ensureDbg→needCp throws → isError /no open game/
specs.surface: src/mcp/tools.js ensureDbg (calls needCp when no dbg) + server catch
specs.setup: createDispatcher({cp:null}) — no cp, no pre-seeded dbg.
