specs.title: Real server keeps stdout pure JSON-RPC; all logging goes to stderr
specs.setup: Spawn `node src/cli.js mcp`; pipe initialize+tools/list; capture stdout and stderr separately.
specs.action: printf '%s\n' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"initialize","params":{}}' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":2,"method":"tools/list"}' | node /path/to/copse/src/cli.js mcp 1>/tmp/copse-mcp.out 2>/tmp/copse-mcp.err; node -e 'const fs=require("fs");const lines=fs.readFileSync("/tmp/copse-mcp.out","utf8").split("\n").filter(Boolean);const bad=lines.filter(l=>{try{JSON.parse(l);return false}catch{return true}});const err=fs.readFileSync("/tmp/copse-mcp.err","utf8");console.log(JSON.stringify({lines:lines.length,badStdout:bad,readyOnStderr:/\[copse-mcp\] ready/.test(err)}))'
specs.groundTruth: server.js redirects console.log/info/debug to stderr and writes only `${JSON.stringify(msg)}\n` to stdout (the 'stdout is the JSON-RPC channel' invariant in docs/MCP.md). VERIFIED THIS SESSION: stdout was a single clean JSON line; the ready line appeared on stderr.
specs.expect: Every stdout line JSON.parses (badStdout===[]), lines===2 (one reply each for initialize+tools/list), and readyOnStderr===true.

specs.title: Server ignores blank and non-JSON stdin lines without crashing
specs.setup: Spawn `copse mcp`; interleave a blank line, garbage, then a valid request.
specs.action: printf '%s\n' '' 'this is not json {{{' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"method":"ping"}' | node /path/to/copse/src/cli.js mcp 2>/dev/null
specs.groundTruth: server.js: `if (!s) return;` for blank; `try { msg = JSON.parse(s) } catch { return }` for non-JSON. Noise produces no reply and no crash. Source.
specs.expect: Exactly one stdout line: {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":5,"result":{}} — blank and garbage lines yielded nothing, the ping still answered.

specs.title: stdin EOF drains the queue and exits 0
specs.setup: Spawn `copse mcp`, send one request, close stdin (the pipe EOF).
specs.groundTruth: server.js: `rl.on('close', () => chain.finally(async () => { ...close cp...; process.exit(0); }))`. With no session, teardown just exits 0. Source.
specs.expect: exit=0 — the process terminates cleanly on EOF (no hang, no non-zero).

specs.title: CLI --debug flag is what flips the advertised set (17↔24)
specs.setup: Spawn both variants; read the stderr ready line which reports the count.
specs.action: for f in '' '--debug'; do printf '%s\n' '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"method":"ping"}' | node /path/to/copse/src/cli.js mcp $f 2>&1 1>/dev/null | head -1; done
specs.groundTruth: cli.js passes `debug: has('--debug')`; server.js ready line is `ready — N tools` (N = advertised count). VERIFIED THIS SESSION: default line says '17 tools (+7 debug, hidden; --debug to show)'.
specs.expect: Without --debug: stderr 'ready — 17 tools (+7 debug, hidden; --debug to show)'. With --debug: 'ready — 24 tools' (no '+N debug, hidden' suffix).

specs.title: `copse mcp --browser-url <url>` does NOT mistake the flag value for the pre-open game URL
specs.setup: Spawn `copse mcp --browser-url http://127.0.0.1:9222`; inspect the stderr ready line.
specs.groundTruth: cli.js: `url = rest.find((a,i)=>/^https?:/.test(a) && !VAL_FLAGS.has(rest[i-1]))` — --browser-url is in VAL_FLAGS, so its value is excluded as the positional url; the server logs 'waiting for connect(url)' not 'pre-opening http://127.0.0.1:9222'. Source guard.
specs.expect: Ready line ends with ', waiting for connect(url)' (url undefined) — the --browser-url value was NOT pre-opened (which would also wrongly launch a browser on startup).

specs.title: get tool transports a distinctively-authored Label value back through JSON-RPC
specs.surface: mcp__copse__get end-to-end (tool → driver → in-page __copse.get → text content)
specs.setup: Author Canvas/ScoreLabel.string="7" via cocos-creator MCP in McpProbe.scene, save; run the preview; mcp__copse__connect({url:'http://localhost:7456/'}).
specs.groundTruth: The value's source is the cocos-creator authoring (mcp__cocos-creator__component_set_component_property / readback of Label.string="7") — an INDEPENDENT oracle. copse merely reads it back over MCP; matching '7' proves the get transport is faithful (not copse echoing its own write).
specs.expect: Result text JSON === {ok:true, value:"7"} — equals the cocos-creator-authored Label.string.

specs.title: press tool dispatches to the live handler and the resulting +1 delta round-trips over MCP
specs.surface: mcp__copse__press + mcp__copse__get (transport reaches the wired handler)
specs.setup: McpProbe.scene running; AddBtn wired to CopseCounter.add (count starts 0, ScoreLabel '0'); connect.
specs.groundTruth: CopseCounter.add increments count by EXACTLY 1 — the fixture script's deterministic source (independent of copse's read). The PREDICTED delta (count: N→N+1) is the oracle, not a copse self-echo. press's {fired,drove} payload must also survive JSON-RPC.
specs.expect: press → {ok:true, fired>=1, drove includes 'clickEvent'}; the second get shows count === first get's count + 1 (predicted delta), proving the MCP transport carried both the action and the read.

specs.title: snapshot tool ships the live tree intact; refs agree with the editor hierarchy
specs.surface: mcp__copse__snapshot (text content carries the node array)
specs.setup: McpProbe.scene running; connect.
specs.groundTruth: cocos-creator mcp__cocos-creator__scene_get_scene_hierarchy is the independent oracle for which nodes exist. Default snapshot must include Canvas/ScoreLabel + Canvas/AddBtn and EXCLUDE Canvas/Panel/CloseBtn (inactive); includeInactive:true must add Canvas/Panel/CloseBtn — matching the editor's active/inactive split.
specs.expect: Default snapshot refs ⊇ {Canvas/ScoreLabel, Canvas/AddBtn} and ∌ Canvas/Panel/CloseBtn; includeInactive snapshot additionally contains Canvas/Panel/CloseBtn — consistent with the cocos-creator hierarchy.

specs.title: Clean flow: steps run in order, judge sees results, no gate trips → pass:true with no gate keys
specs.setup: FAKE driver = localDriver(fixture(),fakeRuntime()) (real copse core, has clickEvents).
specs.action: node: runHarness(FAKE, agent) where agent.plan→{steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn'},{op:'call',sel:'Canvas/Mgr:ShopController.buy',args:[30]},{op:'get',sel:'Canvas/Mgr:ShopController.gold'}]}, agent.judge→({steps})=>({pass: steps.find(s=>s.step.op==='call').result.value===70 && steps.find(s=>s.step.op==='get').result.value===70, scope:'logic'}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: deterministic scripted agent + real core over the fake tree. ShopController.buy(30) on gold 100 = 70 (scripted logic). Harness contract src/harness.js:131-189.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.rounds.length===1; rounds[0].steps[0].result deepEquals {ok:true,ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',fired:1,drove:['clickEvent']}; callR.value===70; getR.value===70; NONE of out.unreachable/errored/undriven/uncertain keys present.

specs.title: reachableGate: press to reachable:false button is a HARD fail, overriding a passing judge AND report
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,reachable:false,blockedBy:'Canvas/Popup/mask'})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: judge AND report both say pass:true; the gate is a FACT (a player can't reach a covered button) that overrides opinion. Contract: out.pass forced false at :185.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.unreachable deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',blockedBy:'Canvas/Popup/mask'}]; rounds[0].steps[0].result.unreachable==='Canvas/Popup/mask'; the handler still fired (rounds[0].steps[0].result.fired===1).

specs.title: reachableGate end-to-end: harness over the REAL puppeteer driver hard-fails pressing CoveredBtn occluded by Overlay
specs.setup: npm run build; preview serving HarnessScene on :7456; Overlay (BlockInputEvents, full-screen, sibling-index after CoveredBtn) covers CoveredBtn.
specs.action: node script: const cp=await connect('http://localhost:7456'); const out=await runHarness(cp,{plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/CoveredBtn'}]}),judge:()=>({pass:true})}); await cp.close(); print out.
specs.groundTruth: cross-tool: cocos-creator mcp__cocos-creator__node_get_node_info confirms Overlay is full-screen, drawn after CoveredBtn, with BlockInputEvents; coir mcp__coir__find confirms CoveredBtn has a wired ClickEvent → reachable:false is independently correct, so the hard-fail is justified (not copse self-reference).
specs.expect: out.pass===false AND out.unreachable[0].ref==='Canvas/CoveredBtn' with blockedBy pointing at the Overlay subtree — matching the editor's geometry + coir's wiring.

specs.title: reachableGate: per-step opts.force:true skips the reachability check entirely (driver.reachable never called)
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable: spy that increments a counter and returns {reachable:false,blockedBy:'X'}}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: the gate guard is `!(step.opts && step.opts.force)`; force is an explicit override. Probe-confirmed reachableCalled===0.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; the reachable spy was called 0 times; 'unreachable' NOT in out; step result has no .unreachable.

specs.title: reachableGate:false disables the gate — out.unreachable is never even computed/attached
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>({ok:true,reachable:false,blockedBy:'Y'})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: gate is skipped in execStep (no .unreachable set), so unreachablePresses stays empty. Asymmetry vs error/drive gates (which still surface). Probe-confirmed.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; 'unreachable' NOT in out (unlike errorGate:false / driveGate:false which still surface their arrays).

specs.title: reachable:false with falsy blockedBy → unreachable surfaced as boolean true (rr.blockedBy || true)
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>({ok:true,reachable:false,blockedBy:null})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: `unreachable = rr.blockedBy || true`. Probe-confirmed out.unreachable=[{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',blockedBy:true}].
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.unreachable deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',blockedBy:true}] (boolean true, not a string).

specs.title: reachable check is press-only: get/call/snapshot/interactive steps never consult driver.reachable
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable: spy returning {reachable:false}}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: the reachable gate lives only inside `case 'press'`. Probe-confirmed reachableCalled===0 for a get step.
specs.expect: reachable spy called 0 times; out.pass===true; no out.unreachable.

specs.title: Gate degrades silently when reachability is unavailable: driver without reachable(), OR localDriver over a Runtime lacking reachable
specs.setup: Case A: driver = FAKE with `delete driver.reachable`. Case B: localDriver(fixture(), fakeRuntime()) where fakeRuntime has NO reachable → coreReachable returns {ok:false,reason:'unsupported'}.
specs.action: node: for each driver, runHarness(driver, {plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn'}]}), judge:()=>({pass:true})}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: A guarded by `typeof driver.reachable==='function'`; B returns reachable:undefined (neither false nor 'unsure') so no signal. Probe-confirmed both pass with no unreachable.
specs.expect: Both cases: out.pass===true; 'unreachable' NOT in out; the press still executed (handler fired).

specs.title: A driver.reachable() that THROWS is best-effort: caught, treated as no signal, press proceeds
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>{throw new Error('boom');}}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: inner `try{...}catch{}` swallows reachability errors (comment 'best-effort'). Probe-confirmed pass true, no unreachable.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; 'unreachable' NOT in out; press result present (no reason:'threw' — only the reachability call threw, not press).

specs.title: reachable:'unsure' is SURFACED as out.uncertain (why:'unsure') but is NOT a hard fail
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>({ok:true,reachable:'unsure',blockedBy:null})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: 'unsure' fails loud for verification, not gospel block. Contract: uncertain set, out.pass unaffected.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.uncertain deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',why:'unsure'}]; rounds[0].steps[0].result.uncertain==='unsure'; NO out.unreachable.

specs.title: reachable + occludedBy (opaque sprite hides it visually) → out.uncertain why:'occluded:<ref>', not hard-failed
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>({ok:true,reachable:true,occludedBy:'Canvas/Img'})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: occludedBy → uncertain=`occluded:${occludedBy}`. Probe-confirmed out.uncertain=[{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',why:'occluded:Canvas/Img'}].
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.uncertain deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',why:'occluded:Canvas/Img'}]; no out.unreachable.

specs.title: Synthetic tap into a no-visible-handler button (drove:['touch'], wired:false) → out.uncertain why:'touch-into-void'
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:0,touched:true,drove:['touch'],wired:false})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: not hard-failed (copse may just not see the touch handler) but surfaced for verification. Contract :101.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.uncertain deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/MaybeDead',why:'touch-into-void'}]; NO out.undriven (drove is ['touch'], not 'nothing').

specs.title: When reachable already set uncertain (occluded/unsure), a drove:['touch']+wired:false does NOT overwrite it
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, reachable:()=>({ok:true,reachable:true,occludedBy:'Canvas/Img'}), press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:0,drove:['touch'],wired:false})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: touch-into-void detection is guarded by `!uncertain`, so the reachability uncertainty wins. Source :101.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.uncertain[0].why==='occluded:Canvas/Img' (NOT 'touch-into-void').

specs.title: driveGate: a press that actuated NOTHING (drove:'nothing') is a HARD fail, overriding judge AND report
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:0,drove:'nothing',wired:false})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: ok:true/fired:0 is the classic misread-as-pass; the gate closes it. Contract: undriven array + out.pass false at :187.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.undriven deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}]; judge+report both returned pass:true yet overall fails.

specs.title: driveGate end-to-end: harness over the real driver hard-fails pressing DeadBtn (Button with no wiring)
specs.setup: DeadBtn = Button with EMPTY clickEvents and no node.on() handler; preview on :7456.
specs.action: node: const cp=await connect('http://localhost:7456'); const out=await runHarness(cp,{plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}]}),judge:()=>({pass:true})}); await cp.close();
specs.groundTruth: cross-tool: coir mcp__coir__find shows DeadBtn has NO ClickEvent edge; cocos-creator component_get_component_info shows Button.clickEvents=[] → the real engine returns drove:'nothing', so the hard-fail is grounded.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.undriven[0].ref==='Canvas/DeadBtn'; consistent with coir/editor showing zero wiring.

specs.title: driveGate:false: out.undriven is still SURFACED (computed regardless) but does not fail the run
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:0,drove:'nothing',wired:false})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: undrivenPresses is computed unconditionally; only the pass-effect is gated by driveGate. Mirrors errorGate asymmetry.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.undriven STILL deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/DeadBtn'}] (surfaced even with the gate off).

specs.title: errorGate: a press whose result carries errors[] (engine-swallowed throw via log-diff) is a HARD fail over judge AND report
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:1,errors:[{level:'error',text:"TypeError: cannot read 'x' of undefined"}]})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: ok:true hides an engine-swallowed throw; the log-diff errors make 'doesn't-crash' a fact. Contract: errored + out.pass false at :186.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.errored[0].ref==='Canvas/ShopBtn'; out.errored[0].error matches /TypeError/ (errors[0].text); overall fails despite judge+report pass:true.

specs.title: errorGate: a step whose driver call THREW (reason:'threw') is captured AND hard-failed
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:()=>{throw new Error('kaboom');}}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: execStep catches → {ok:false,reason:'threw',error}; errorGate filters reason==='threw' using s.result.error as the message. Probe-confirmed out.errored=[{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',error:'kaboom'}].
specs.expect: out.pass===false; rounds[0].steps[0].result deepEquals {ok:false,reason:'threw',error:'kaboom'}; out.errored deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',error:'kaboom'}].

specs.title: errorGate:false: out.errored still SURFACED but the run is not failed
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:(ref)=>({ok:true,ref,fired:1,errors:[{level:'error',text:'TypeError x'}]})}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: erroredSteps computed unconditionally; only `(!errorGate || ...)` and the final `if(errorGate && ...)` gate the pass-effect. Probe-confirmed pass true, errored still present.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.errored STILL deepEquals [{ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn',error:'TypeError x'}].

specs.title: errorGate is op-agnostic and its ref falls back step.ref → step.sel → step.op (e.g. a call step that threw uses sel)
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, call:()=>{throw new Error('nope');}}.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: a call step has no ref; `ref: s.step.ref || s.step.sel || s.step.op` → the sel. errorGate filters ANY op (not just press) with errors[]/threw.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.errored[0].ref==='Canvas/Mgr:ShopController.buy'; out.errored[0].error==='nope'.

specs.title: errorGate end-to-end: pressing ThrowBtn whose handler throws surfaces result.errors via the puppeteer log-diff → hard fail
specs.setup: ThrowBtn → Thrower.boom() throws new Error('copse-harn-boom'); preview on :7456.
specs.action: node: const cp=await connect('http://localhost:7456'); const out=await runHarness(cp,{plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/ThrowBtn'}]}),judge:()=>({pass:true})}); await cp.close();
specs.groundTruth: delta: the Thrower.boom source throws a KNOWN string 'copse-harn-boom'; the driver's CDP console/pageerror capture is the independent oracle that the throw happened (not copse self-report). Honest caveat: depends on the engine routing the clickEvent throw to console.error/pageerror.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.errored[0].ref==='Canvas/ThrowBtn'; out.errored[0].error contains 'copse-harn-boom'. (If the engine fully swallows the throw with no log, document as a known surfacing gap.)

specs.title: A throwing step + an unknown-op step are captured (not fatal); the judge can still fail on their results
specs.setup: driver = {...FAKE, press:()=>{throw new Error('boom');}}.
specs.action: node: runHarness(driver, {plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn'},{op:'frobnicate'}]}), judge:({steps})=>({pass: steps.every(s=>s.result.ok!==false), reason:'no errors'})}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: loop continues past a throw; both steps recorded. Note out.pass would already be false via errorGate on reason:'threw' — assert the loop survived + recorded both.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; rounds[0].steps.length===2; steps[0].result deepEquals {ok:false,reason:'threw',error:'boom'}; steps[1].result.reason==='unknown-op'.

specs.title: An unrecognized step.op returns {ok:false,reason:'unknown-op',op} and is not itself a hard-fail (only judge can fail it)
specs.setup: FAKE driver.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: default switch arm; no gate keys reference unknown-op. Probe-confirmed out.pass true (judge controls), result={ok:false,reason:'unknown-op',op:'frobnicate'}.
specs.expect: out.pass===true (judge said pass, no gate triggers); rounds[0].steps[0].result deepEquals {ok:false,reason:'unknown-op',op:'frobnicate'}.

specs.title: execStep routes get/call/snapshot/interactive to the matching driver method; call spreads args
specs.setup: counting driver = FAKE wrapped so each method records its invocation + args.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: get→driver.get(sel); call→driver.call(sel,...args); snapshot→driver.snapshot(opts); interactive→driver.interactive(). buy(40) over gold 100 = 60.
specs.expect: get result.value===100; call result.value===60 (args spread); snapshot called with {relevant:true} and returns an array; interactive returns an array; out.pass===true.

specs.title: agent.next:{continue:true} drives iteration, hard-bounded by maxRounds; re-snapshots between rounds
specs.setup: counting driver wraps FAKE.snapshot to count calls.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: next always wants more, maxRounds is the bound. Re-snapshot only when round<maxRounds-1 → 1 initial + rounds 0,1 = 3.
specs.expect: out.rounds.length===3 (capped despite next:true); snapshot call count===3.

specs.title: agent.next absent ⇒ exactly one round regardless of maxRounds
specs.setup: FAKE driver; agent has NO next.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: `if(!agent.next) break;` after round 0. Default policy = one round.
specs.expect: out.rounds.length===1 (not 5).

specs.title: agent.next returning {continue:false} OR a falsy value stops the loop after the current round
specs.setup: FAKE driver. Case A next:()=>({continue:false}); Case B next:()=>null.
specs.action: node: for each, runHarness(FAKE, {plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'interactive'}]}), judge:()=>({pass:true}), next}, {maxRounds:4}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: `if (!cont || !cont.continue) break;` — both null and {continue:false} stop.
specs.expect: Both cases: out.rounds.length===1.

specs.title: Re-discovery only happens if another round will run: no extra snapshot before the final (cap) round
specs.setup: counting driver wraps FAKE.snapshot.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: guard `if (round < maxRounds-1) snapshot=...`. With maxRounds:2 → 1 initial + 1 re-snap (after round0) = 2 (round1 is last → no re-snap).
specs.expect: snapshot call count===2 (1 initial + 1 between rounds, none after the capped round).

specs.title: maxRounds:0 → zero rounds run; per-round AND has rounds.length===0 → overall pass:false
specs.setup: FAKE driver.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: the for-loop never enters; `pass = rounds.length>0 && ...` → false. Probe-confirmed pass:false, rounds:0.
specs.expect: out.pass===false; out.rounds.length===0; the initial snapshot is still present as out.snapshot.

specs.title: A verdict with no/undefined pass counts as pass (per-round AND uses verdict.pass !== false)
specs.setup: FAKE driver; judge returns {} (no pass field).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: `every(r => r.verdict && r.verdict.pass !== false)` — only an explicit false fails. Probe-confirmed pass:true.
specs.expect: out.pass===true (an absent verdict.pass is NOT treated as failure).

specs.title: opts.context is passed verbatim to every stage (plan, judge, next, report)
specs.setup: FAKE driver; each stage records the ctx.context it received into a shared array.
specs.action: node: const ctx={goal:'g',diff:'d'}; runHarness(FAKE, {plan:rec, judge:rec, next:()=>({continue:false})&&rec, report:rec}, {context:ctx, maxRounds:1}) where rec captures ctx.context.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: harness is prompt-agnostic; each stage receives {context,...}. Same object identity passed through.
specs.expect: plan, judge, next, and report each received ctx.context === the SAME {goal:'g',diff:'d'} object (deepEqual + identity).

specs.title: agent.report returning {pass,summary} sets the OVERALL verdict + summary, overriding the per-round AND
specs.setup: FAKE driver; judge:()=>({pass:true}); report:()=>({pass:false,summary:{fmt:'mine'}}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: `if ('pass' in rep) { out.pass=rep.pass; out.summary=rep.summary; }`. The report alone sees the whole goal, so it is authoritative absent a hard gate.
specs.expect: out.pass===false (report overrode passing rounds); out.summary deepEquals {fmt:'mine'}.

specs.title: agent.report returning a plain (non-{pass}) value is treated as the summary only; overall pass stays the per-round AND
specs.setup: FAKE driver; judge:()=>({pass:true}); report:()=>'just text'.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: else-branch `out.summary = rep`. Probe-confirmed pass:true, summary:'just text'.
specs.expect: out.pass===true; out.summary==='just text'.

specs.title: No report stage ⇒ out has no summary key at all
specs.setup: FAKE driver; agent has plan+judge only.
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: the report block is skipped when agent.report is absent.
specs.expect: ('summary' in out)===false.

specs.title: A report returning {pass:true} CANNOT pass over an active hard gate (unreachable/error/drive re-force false AFTER report)
specs.setup: Three drivers, each tripping one gate, all with report:()=>({pass:true,summary:'ok'}): (a) reachable:false; (b) errors[]; (c) drove:'nothing'.
specs.action: node: for each driver, runHarness(driver, {plan:()=>({steps:[{op:'press',ref:'Canvas/ShopBtn'}]}), judge:()=>({pass:true}), report:()=>({pass:true,summary:'ok'})}).
specs.groundTruth: structural-invariant: lines 185-187 re-set out.pass=false AFTER the report block — the gate is a fact the report cannot overturn. unreachable is unconditional; error/drive guarded by their gates (default on).
specs.expect: All three: out.pass===false even though report returned {pass:true}; out.summary==='ok' is still set; the respective out.unreachable / out.errored / out.undriven is present.

specs.title: agent.report receives ctx.pass = the gate-adjusted out.pass AND ctx.unreachable = the offending refs

fixtureAsks.purpose: Single de-identified fixture scene authored via cocos-creator MCP exercising every READ-lens branch: slim/relevant/includeInactive/components snapshot flags, ref [i] disambiguation, button/label fields, clickSurface rows + method:null, get path:Comp.prop & path:Node.prop, node intrinsics, listeners. Opened in the editor, saved, and the copse-driven preview reloaded (mcp__copse__reload) so copse sees it. coir reads the SAME .scene file statically (independent oracle); cocos-creator node/component_get_*_info reads the editor scene (independent oracle).
components: Canvas (cc.Canvas, cc.UITransform) — root container, must surface as top-level ref 'Canvas' (scene root is NOT a path segment)
Canvas/ScoreLabel (cc.Label string="0", fontSize=40, cc.UITransform) — Label.string/fontSize authored-readback + snapshot label field
Canvas/AddBtn (cc.Button interactable=true; clickEvents=[{target:Counter, component:CopseCounter, handler:"add", customEventData:"1"}]) — single-clickEvent row, interactable:true, method join to coir
Canvas/TwoEventBtn (cc.Button interactable=true; clickEvents=[{handler:"add"},{handler:"reset"}]) — two rows same ref, distinct methods
Canvas/TouchBtn (cc.Button interactable=true; clickEvents=[]) — empty clickEvents → clickSurface method:null row
Canvas/Counter (CopseCounter count=5) — get custom component field + clickEvent handler target
Canvas/Item (plain node + cc.Label string="second") — same-name sibling [1], distinguishable value for [i] readback
Canvas/Panel (active=false; cc.UITransform) — includeInactive + Node.active false authored-readback
